title: Artist Talk and Demo with Guest Artist Jason Ward
Lancaster Breakfast and History Walking Tour
location: Unique Lancaster, 23 N Walnut Street, Lancaster, 17603, United States
description:
Searching for a walking history tour in Lancaster? You’re in for a flavorful experience—literally! 


Introducing the Lancaster History & Breakfast Walking Tour—a dynamic two-hour adventure that blends the rich heritage of one of America’s oldest inland cities with a delicious morning meal. Explore Lancaster’s vibrant streets with a professionally trained local guide and journey through time from the early 1700s through the transformative decades of the 20th century. 


Brought to you by Unique Lancaster Experiences, this guided walk kicks off with your choice of hot or iced coffee or tea to energize your morning. Along the way, you’ll hear engaging stories about Lancaster’s evolution as a bustling Market City and its transition from trolleys to automobiles after WWI. 


But this isn’t your standard history tour. You’ll dive into interactive discussions and uncover captivating insights about colonial life, architectural landmarks, and the impact of prohibition on the region. 


As you explore, you’ll stop at five handpicked local spots to enjoy a curated tasting menu featuring hearty breakfast sandwiches, fresh fruits, sweet pastries, and other morning favorites. 


Please note: This tour offers vegetarian-friendly options only. If you have other dietary needs, we recommend our International Food Tour for a better fit. 


Whether you’re passionate about history, love discovering new flavors, or just want a memorable way to see the city, the Lancaster History & Breakfast Tour is a great choice for solo explorers, couples, and groups alike.

title: Backstreet Brothel Historic Walking Tour
location: Lancaster City Welcome Center, 38 Penn Square, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
On this history tour, walk the streets and alleys of Lancaster with an expert guide and learn about the brothel keepers, bartenders, and pool hall hustlers who shared the streets with corrupt politicians and righteous reformers a century ago. 



Discover the history of wage-earning women, Lancaster nightlife, and changing sexual values through the stories of the men and women of Lancaster’s red-light districts.

title: Savage Cabaret: Electric Parlor
location: West Art Community Center, 816 Buchanan Ave, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, 17603, United States
description:
Experience burlesque, drag and comedy as they take over City Hall at West Art, where stained glass windows and soaring wooden beams set the stage for an unforgettable evening of glamour, expression and mischief. 



This special edition of Savage Cabaret brings the magic closer than ever before. With only 45 small tables per show, audiences will experience vaudeville the way it was meant to be seen — intimate, up close and personal. 



Each one-hour performance features a dazzling lineup of consisting of 3 burlesque artists, one drag queen and one songstress, creating a fast-paced, high-energy cabaret experience that is as playful as it is intense. 



Whether you’re planning a group night out, a memorable date, or simply looking for something extraordinary to do in Lancaster, Savage Cabaret: Electric Parlor offers a night unlike anything else in Central Pennsylvania. 



Tables seat two guests with the option to add up to two more chairs. 



Only 45 tables are available per show. Once they’re gone, they’re gone. 



Reserve yours now and get ready to feel the buzz. 



Friday Night Lineup: 



Hosted by  Wilfredo (NYC) 



Burlesque by London St. Juniper (DC) 



Burlesque by Kalypso Kraken (MD) 



Hula from Caresse Deville (PHL)

title: Unforgettable Fire
location: Mickey’s Black Box, 101 Rock Lititz Boulevard, Lititz, PA, 17543, United States
description:
Saturday April 4th, 2026
Doors | 7:00 PM
Show | 8:00 PM 
General Admission Standing Room | $33.55 + Fees

+$5 on All Day of Show Purchases
This event is all ages – 21+ With Valid ID to Drink
Come hungry! Snacks and light bites will be available at the canteen.
—————————————————
Presented by Big Fan Presents
—————————————————
Unforgettable Fire, or ‘UF’, was born on New Year’s Day in 1995 as one of the very first U2 Tribute Bands to ever perform in America. After twenty five years of performing at the most prestigious venues throughout the East Coast and beyond, UF have formed the reputation of being the preeminent U2 Tribute Show in North America. 
The band strives to present a truly authentic U2 experience, both sonically, and visually. In addition to performing a very impressive array of U2 material from pre-”Boy” to the most recent “Songs of Experience”, the band does everything in their power (from wardrobe to lighting) to create the live U2 concert setting for their audience. The members of UF are also very dedicated fans of U2, and will not settle for anything less than complete authenticity. 
In 2015, U2 members The Edge and Adam Clayton, crashed Unforgettable Fire’s show at The Cutting Room in New York City. They joined UF onstage for a very special surprise performance together to the joy and surprise of the audience. Adam Clayton and The Edge performed two songs with Unforgettable Fire; “Out Of Control” and “Where The Streets Have No Name”. Press from around the world, including Rolling Stone Magazine reported on that very special U2 and UF performance together. 
2021 brought new worldwide exposure for the band, including an episode of JEOPARDY!, in which Unforgettable Fire were featured as a question, in a category titled ‘Tribute Bands’. In October 2021 Unforgettable Fire were featured & appeared on the series premiere of “Clash of The Cover Bands” on ‘E!’ Network, executive produced by Jimmy Fallon, with judges Adam Lambert, Meghan Trainor, and Ester Dean. UF won their episode with much praise and acclaim from the celebrity judges. 
Check out Unforgettable Fire here! 
Supported by: Zoey Noble

location: Lancaster Improv Players, 16 S Prince St, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, 17603, United States
description:

Hilarious games and silly scenes abound in this monthly improv showcase from Lancaster Improv Players’ legendary house team PEACH SODA. BYOB 



$15 at the door 



The improv jam typically begins around 10:00pm. 



Due to the size of our theater, there is a quick turnover time between our scheduled shows. If you purchase tickets to both events on Saturday evening, you will be asked to wait outside during the break so that the next team can warm up before the audience returns. 



ABOUT PEACH SODA 



Peach Soda combines short form improv games and long form improv scenes that keep you on the edge of your seat! To ensure every performance is one of a kind, the focus of the team shifts every few months to learn new skills so the flavor is always fresh. They even recently added new team members! title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

title: Competitive Ride (A+) - CV P&PRAEWMF Ride (34VH)
description:
Lancaster Bicycle Club (LBC) Meetup
Anticipated average pace: **20+ mph**

Cue and GPX link: [https://ridewithgps.com/routes/30097162](https://ridewithgps.com/routes/30097162)

**This ride is intended for the experienced group rider in good physical condition and capable of maintaining a steady pace. Few stops if any. Riders unable to maintain the pace may be dropped. If unsure if this ride is for you, it is recommended you try the "A" ride option first.**

Helmets are mandatory. No earbuds. Please bring water and a basic repair kit (spare tube, tire levers, pump/CO2 inflator).

To eliminate passing around a pen and paper waiver, we require an LBC Club membership and Meetup RSVP below to join this ride.

title: Intermediate Ride (C) - EP Landisville Casual (22F)
description:
Lancaster Bicycle Club (LBC) Meetup
**Be Safe, Have Fun, and Ride Together**

Ride leader: open
Anticipated Average Pace: (14-16 mph)
Route: [https://routes.lancasterbikeclub.net/cue/ep-landisville-casual-ride-22f/](https://routes.lancasterbikeclub.net/cue/ep-landisville-casual-ride-22f/)

This is an Intermediate C Class ride. The ride leader will ride at the advertised average speed. This is intended to be a group ride with a no-drop rule as the main goal. The ride leader will do his/her best to keep the group together with occasional regroupings and/or splitting into multiple groups to align to pace capabilities.

Please do not join this ride if you are not willing to ride with the group. If you exceed the posted pace, you will be riding by yourself and encouraged to join a faster group. If maintaining a 14-16 mph average is beyond your current ability level, please consider joining a slower ride first.

Helmets are mandatory. No earbuds. Please bring water and a basic repair kit (spare tube, tire levers, pump/CO2 inflator).

To eliminate passing around a pen and paper waiver, we require LBC Club membership and Meetup RSVP below to join this ride. Being on Meetup does not mean you are a club member. Join here [Member Signup](https://www.lancasterbikeclub.net/content.aspx?page_id=60&club_id=257148)

title: Children's Chapel
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Children’s Chapel* meets weekly during the 10:30 AM Sunday Mass. Children and youth ages 3 and above begin the morning in the sanctuary where they can experience the processional and the opening congregational prayers. 
After the opening Collect, children are invited to the front of the sanctuary to make their way to the chapel and participate in songs, prayers, brief meditation and storytelling of the day’s Gospel and/or New and Old Testament readings. 
The Godly Play® teaching method includes using beautiful wooden figures, candles, and symbolic colors to aid our storytelling as we listen to God’s word for us. Children often respond to the story using simple art supplies including crayons, construction paper, origami paper and Model Magic air dry clay. 
Children return to the Sanctuary during the passing of the Peace. They are welcome to take part in Holy Communion.

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

title: Fact vs Fiction: The Reality of a Missing Persons Investigation
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center B
description:
Community, Presentation, Adults, Seniors

We've all read it in novels and watched it on TV: a person vanishes and a lone detective solves the case in 48 hours. But what actually happens when a Pennsylvanian goes missing? Join us for an evening with State Trooper James Grothey and officers from the Manheim Township Police Department as they pull back the curtain on the real-world investigative process.From the critical first hours and to the role of digital forensics and community alerts, learn how law enforcement navigates the complexities of search and recovery. Discover the truth behind "The First 48," the legal hurdles of the job, and how real life is much more intricate than fiction. Registration is encouraged

We’ll discuss the profound impact these dogs have on those they visit, as well as the dedication required from their handlers.

title: CoffeeTue- IC Houston Cafe (22R) -- RSVP required
description:
Lancaster Bicycle Club (LBC) Meetup
Ride Leader: Bruce Black

CoffeeTue rides:
• A rotating start location, not a school
• A predetermined route
• 25+/- mile routes
• 13 to 15 mph average pace
• Usually no sweep
• “short” stop for refreshment/coffee mid-route at [Houston Cafe](https://www.thehoustoncafe.com/)

Click for [Ride with GPS](https://routes.lancasterbikeclub.net/cue/__trashed/)

Helmets are mandatory. No ear buds. Please bring water and a basic repair kit (spare tube, tire levers, pump/CO2 inflator).

To eliminate passing around a pen and paper waiver, we require LBC Club membership and Meetup RSVP below to join this ride.

Being on Meetup does not mean you are a club member. [Join here](https://www.lancasterbikeclub.net/content.aspx?page_id=60&club_id=257148).

Your RSVP is your agreement to abide by the club's [Code of Conduct](https://lancbikeclub.clubexpress.com/content.aspx?page_id=22&club_id=257148&module_id=323984) ,
[Rules of the Road](https://lancbikeclub.clubexpress.com/content.aspx?page_id=22&club_id=257148&module_id=325159) , and [Ride Waiver](https://lancasterbikeclub.net/docs.ashx?id=454318)

location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Storytime, Children

Join Ms. Laura for a fun and engaging preschool program designed to spark curiosity and a love for learning in young children! This program features stories, songs, and hands-on activities tailored for preschool-aged kids. Each session focuses on building early literacy, motor skills, and social interaction through playful exploration.Perfect for ages 3–6 with an adult, this program provides a welcoming and inclusive environment where kids can learn and grow together. Parents and caregivers are encouraged to participate and share in the joy of discovery with their little ones.Ms. Laura has over 20 years of preschool classroom experience bringing a wealth of knowledge in early childhood development. Her approach focuses on creating a warm, engaging, and supportive environment where children can thrive.Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, March 25th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, March 27th for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Please plan to attend and be engaged with your child. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.Preschool Party Registration Requirements – All children who will be participating in the craft will need to be registered as a participant to allow for adequate supplies.

title: Ignatian Spirituality
location: Saint James Parish House Library
description:
Ignatian Spirituality
Tuesday evenings from 5:15 – 6:15 PM
September 9 – April 28, 2026
Ignatian spirituality is a way of living spirituality in everyday life. It asserts that God is present in our world and actively involved in our lives. It is a pathway to deeper prayer, good decisions guided by keen discernment and an active life of service to others. 
This 34-week program is designed for individuals who wish to commit to personal transformation by encountering God and deepening their experience of the divine. It will involve a daily and weekly commitment to spiritual practices with help and support from spiritual director Pat Latshaw. 
The book, The Ignatian Adventure by Kevin O’Brien will be used for discussion and to guide the group’s approach to spiritual growth with techniques that have been in use for almost 500 years. 
The cost is $875 for 34 sessions. Scholarships are available. 
Have questions about scholarships and/or Ignatian Spirituality? Click here to email Pat Latshaw. 
Click here to register for the course.

title: Revolution: The Music of the Beatles—A Symphonic Experience
location: Lancaster County Convention Center, 25 S Queen St, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Experience The Beatles like never before as the LSO joins top vocalists and rock musicians to perform sweeping orchestral arrangements of more than 25 of the Fab Four’s top hits. Revolution immerses audiences in legendary songs like Ticket to Ride, Penny Lane, All You Need Is Love, Get Back, Here Comes the Sun, and Hey Jude (and many more)—accompanied by stunning video, animation, and hundreds of rare and unseen photos from the archives of the band’s official fan magazine, The Beatles Book Monthly. 



Grammy-winner Jeff Tyzik’s new arrangements, transcribed and arranged from the original master recordings at Abbey Road, bring The Beatles’ magic to life in this celebration of the band’s enduring legacy.

title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

Wednesdays with the Saints
Click here  to join Wednesday Evening Prayer. (This link can be used for every Wednesday Evening Prayer on Zoom. Passcode=WedEP) 

Yours is the day, O God, yours also the night; you established the moon and the sun. You fixed all the boundaries of the earth; you made both summer and winter. Psalm 74:15,16 

I will bless the Lord who gives me counsel; my heart teaches me, night after night. I have set the Lord always before me; because he is at my right hand, I shall not fall. Psalm 16:7,8 

Daily prayers are said on a regular schedule at Saint James. On Wednesday evenings, we invite you to explore and celebrate the inspiring lives of the saints at our Evening Prayer service. title: The Making of "The Quiet Gospel"
location: Milanof-Schock
description:
Community, Discussion, Presentation, Adults

Join Mount Joy native and local author Rodney Garber for a collaborative discussion and book signing of his book The Quiet Gospel.The book outlines 21 principles for living and loving like Jesus in a loud and offended world, inviting readers to consider a simple but powerful question:What if the loudest way to share our faith was simply to live it quietly?During the event, Rodney will also share the story behind the book—how it came to be written, the unexpected journey of putting it together, and the ups and downs of writing a book he never originally intended to write.Through reflection and practical insight, Rodney explores how character, consistency, humility, and everyday interactions can reflect the life and example of Christ. Rather than focusing on louder voices, The Quiet Gospel points to a quieter, relational approach to faith—one lived out through how we treat people and engage the world around us.The event will include a short discussion, time for questions and conversation, and an opportunity to meet the author and have a copy of the book signed.Registration Required

title: LBC Bike to 30K Program: Bike Check Up with Trek Bikes
description:
Lancaster Bicycle Club (LBC) Meetup
The Bike to 30K is hosting a Bike Check up with Trek Bikes! Bring your bike to Trek Bikes for a service consult.

**Grab your helmets, bikes, and sneakers — Lancaster Bicycle Club is gearing up for beginner and transitional rides!** Local members of the LBC are thrilled to host this year’s **Bike to 30K Program**, designed especially for new, returning, and curious cyclists.

## **About the Program**

Bike to 30K is a seven‑week series that gently builds your confidence, fitness, and mileage. We begin with a five‑mile ride and gradually work up to eighteen miles by the end of the program. Our goal is simple: to introduce riders of all experience levels to the joy of cycling in a supportive, community‑focused environment.
This program is **FREE** with an LBC membership — and membership is also free. The Bike to 30K program is open to everyone in our community who wants to ride.

## **Why We Ride**

Ever wonder why cyclists keep coming back? Because it’s fun. There’s nothing like coasting downhill and letting out a big “weeeeeee” all the way to the bottom. But we also know that if you’re new to cycling, jumping straight into an 18‑mile ride can feel intimidating. That’s exactly why Bike to 30K exists.
Each week, you’ll increase your mileage at a comfortable pace. You’ll get to know the club, meet fellow riders, and gradually grow into a confident **Fun Rider** — the group known for enjoying scenic, social rides together.

## **Our Commitment to You**

Lancaster Bicycle Club is dedicated to supporting new riders and helping our community reach their cycling goals. Bike to 30K is part of our initiative to encourage more people to get outside, get active, and discover how enjoyable cycling can be.
Spend seven weeks with us and watch yourself transform into a Fun Rider — one mile at a time.
The Bike to 30K program is voluntary, welcoming, and designed with beginners in mind. We can’t wait to ride with you.

## **Membership, Waiver & RSVP Requirements**

To eliminate passing around a pen‑and‑paper waiver, we require both:

* **LBC Club Membership**, and
* **Meetup RSVP**

Your RSVP allows us to contact you after the ride if needed. Please note: **ONLY club members who RSVP are covered by the club’s excess accident insurance policy.** Being on Meetup does *not* mean you are a club member. To join our wonderful club, please sign up through our membership link.

title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

Public On-Sale: Friday, August 15 at 10:00 a.m. 
Often referred to as American Music Royalty, The Temptations are world-renowned superstars of entertainment, revered for their phenomenal catalog of music and prolific career.  Thanks to their fine-tuned choreography, and even finer harmonies, The Temptations became one of the most successful acts to record for Motown Records.  During the group’s six-decade career, these Grammy winners have charted numerous #1 hits including “My Girl,” “Ain’t Too Proud to Beg,” “Get Ready,” “Just My Imagination,” and “Papa Was a Rollin’ Stone,” among many others. 
The Four Tops teamed up in high school and spent over four decades without a single personnel change to become one of Motown’s most consistent hit makers.  “Baby I Need Your Loving,” “I Can’t Help Myself (Sugar Pie, Honey Bunch),” “Reach Out (I’ll Be There),” “Standing in the Shadows of Love,” and “Ain’t No Woman (Like the One I’ve Got)” are just some of their classic hits. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

title: Court Appointed Special Advocates (CASA) Community Awareness Breakfast
location: DoubleTree Resort, 2400 Willow Street Pike, Lancaster, 17602
description:
Event Details

Join Court Appointed Special Advocates (CASA) for breakfast to learn how to participate in the critical advocacy work CASA provides for children who have experienced abuse and neglect and are placed in the foster care system.  

Hear from CASA staff and supporters about the crucial and life-changing impact CASA volunteers have on the lives of our community’s most vulnerable children.  

The Community Awareness Breakfast is open to anyone who wants to learn more about becoming a CASA volunteer, donor, or friend. Guests will be given the opportunity to make a contribution. 

This event is free and open to the public, but registration is required. Please feel free to bring a friend! Please register by March 30, 2026.

Questions? Please contact raquel@casalancleb.org 


Who We Are 

CASA of Lancaster and Lebanon Counties recruits and trains citizen volunteers to advocate for the best interests of youth who have suffered abuse and neglect and are in the foster care system. 

We have served over 1,100 of Lancaster and Lebanon County’s most vulnerable children since our program began in 2009. 

Our mission is to provide a qualified and compassionate court-appointed volunteer advocate to every child who is abused and neglected, to ensure the fundamental human right of having a safe, nurturing, and permanent home is met. 

To learn more, please visit www.casalancleb.org

title: Together, They Made the Modern American Presidency
location: Quarryville, QVL C.X. Carlson Cultural Room
description:
Presentation, Teens, Adults, Seniors

Adult program, older teens are welcome. Please register for each session.Dr. Robert Frick, local historian, author, and retired Lampeter Strasburg School District Superintendent, has developed a number of lecture series to inform and entertain you with objective, non-partisan content.  This series focuses on the modern American Presidency.The framers of the U.S. Constitution were wary of the Executive Branch, the most likely source of tyranny in the government. Having escaped the authority of a king, they wanted no more of it or an executive who would dominate the new government; thus, the careful wording of Article II. As leaders, Presidents need to communicate with the public, build a team, be assertive, create overarching goals, process information, and manage their emotions. These qualities come in varying degrees and sundry types with each new President. Historians believe it takes 20 years to properly evaluate a presidency, therefore this series culminates with the 43rd president.February 27 –  FDR, TrumanMarch 6 – Eisenhower, KennedyMarch 13 –  Johnson, NixonMarch 20 –  Ford, CarterMarch 27 – Reagan, GHW BushApril 3 - Library closed April 10 – Clinton, GW Bush

Join us on Friday, April 10th as we create, test, and evaluate our very own Wind Cars. description:
A community-wide conspiracy designed to bring laughter to Lancaster

GO BULLDOGSIt’s tradition around these parts that whenever you hear someone say “Go Bulldogs!”, you have to reply “GO BULLDOGS!” GBD is Lancaster Improv Players longest running team; each show is a randomized selection of players from our entire community of students, coaches, and performers. that means once you finish an improv course at LIP, you can be a Bulldog, too! 



BYOB & Pay What You Will 



Doors at 6:45PM, show at 7:00PM 



Due to the size of our theater, there is a quick turnover time between our scheduled shows. If you purchase tickets to both events on Friday evening, you will be asked to wait outside during the break so that the next team can warm up before the audience returns. 



LANCASTER IMPROV PLAYERS is an intimate black box venue located in the heart of Lancaster City. With a capacity of approximately 30 guests, that means this Lancaster comedy club is entirely front row. Not a nosebleed in sight! After all, BYOB is just a drink special you never get tired of.

title: Video Game Day
location: The Lancaster Science Factory, 454 New Holland Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Game on! 
We’re partnering with the Lancaster Rec Esports program for a day of video game design and play! Compete in popular games like Super Smash Brothers™ and Mario Kart™. Players can test their skills, cheer on friends, and experience the excitement of esports in a welcoming, supportive environment.

Visitors of all ages can also participate in our Play for Impact Video Game Design Workshops. Participants will design, develop, and test their own video game using Scratch, a block-based programming tool.

Stay tuned for more video game fun as the event gets closer!  This event is presented free with admission.  
Click here to purchase tickets in advance for April 11th. Tickets will also be sold at the door.
Why Video Games and Esports?
Esports is one of the fastest-growing career fields in the world, offering opportunities not only as players but also in areas like computer programming, digital art, broadcasting, marketing, and game development. Colleges and universities, including local institutions, now offer esports teams, scholarships, and academic pathways that turn a passion for gaming into meaningful careers.

title: Spring Fling at Lancaster Friends School
location: Lancaster Friends School, 1210 Maple Avenue, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Spring is in the air, and that means it’s time for our favorite tradition: the Annual Spring Fling! Join us on April 11th at 1210 Maple Avenue in Lancaster from 11am-3pm for a day full of laughter, live music, local food, and family fun – all in support of our mission of nurturing a peace-centered and socially conscious learning community. This year’s Spring Fling will feature: Local vendors and artisans showcasing unique, handmade goods Live performances from area musicians and dance groups Games and activities for kids of all ages Delicious food from community favorites Silent auction Whether you’ve joined us in the past or you’re a first-time guest, Spring Fling is the perfect way to connect with friends, neighbors, and our mission of nurturing a peace-centered and socially conscious learning community. Admission is free, and all proceeds from our silent auction and ticketed activities go directly to funding educational programs and outreach initiatives that strengthen our community.Bring your family, invite your friends, and help us make this year’s celebration our biggest yet! Founded by members of the local Quaker community, Lancaster Friends School was established to offer education rooted in the belief that “education is the way to lasting peace, and there can be no peace without justice.” LFS embraces the Quaker testimonies of Simplicity, Peace, Integrity, Community, Equality/Equity, and Stewardship/Sustainability, known collectively as SPICES, which guide both its educational philosophy and day-to-day practice. LFS serves children from preschool through eighth grade, fostering an environment where curiosity, compassion, and conscience grow together in balance. 




    Learn More

title: Understanding Immigration Law: Naturalization
location: Quarryville, QVL C.X. Carlson Cultural Room
description:
Discussion, Presentation, Adults

Registration is preferred; walk-ins will be accommodated as space allows. Best for adults and older interested teens.We welcome local immigration attorney Paige Britton, who will share her professional experience working within the U.S. immigration system over several sessions, exploring a different element each month.Naturalization – How does someone become a citizen?Learn about the pathways and possibilities for noncitizens to become U.S. citizens under American immigration law. Who may naturalize, and can you pass the civics test??This event is offered solely as information about the American legal system and should not be construed as legal advice. If you need legal counsel on this or other immigration matters, please consult a qualified immigration attorney.

title: An Evening of Alton Brown
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $64 $74 $84 
VIP: Alton Brown VIP Experience: $200 
Includes: 

One ticket for admission
A special VIP experience one hour before show time.
It will include a brief Q&A session in the Theatre.
A signed copy of Alton’s book.

*There will be no individual photos or autographs, however, photos will be allowed during the experience.  Video is strictly prohibited. 
*If you purchase a VIP Experience, AMT will notify you by email prior to the event to give you additional instructions. 
Although he’s keeping the details under wraps, Brown has said that with An Evening of Alton Brown he’s “stirring up some twists on his greatest hits,” and “spicing things up with new demonstrations, music, and slightly scaled-down mayhem.”  No matter what, An Evening of Alton Brown will present Brown as you’ve never seen him — unless, of course, you’ve been invited to his house. 
Best known for creating and hosting groundbreaking television such as Good Eats, Iron Chef America, Cutthroat Kitchen, and Food Network Star, Brown has also built one of the most successful culinary touring franchises ever, performing live in more than 300 cities for well over half a million fans. With An Evening of Alton Brown, he strips away the spectacle in favor of connection, spontaneity, and storytelling, delivering a show that is equal parts humor, science, food, and heart. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Nitty Gritty Dirt Band - All the Good Times: The Farewell Tour - 60 Years of Dirt
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $79
 
Nitty Gritty Dirt Band has been delighting audiences with their signature blend of country, folk, rock, and roots music. Widely regarded as one of the most influential bands in American roots history, their career spans multiple generations and includes timeless hits like “Mr. Bojangles,” “Fishin’ in the Dark,” “An American Dream,” and “House at Pooh Corner.” 
The All the Good Times Tour celebrates the band’s incredible legacy — from their groundbreaking Will the Circle Be Unbroken collaborations to their chart-topping radio favorites. With their trademark harmonies, masterful musicianship, and heartfelt storytelling, The Nitty Gritty Dirt Band continues to prove that great music only gets better with age. 
Joining NGDB is emerging country artist Meels with her soulful voice, heartfelt lyrics, and modern country flair. Blending authentic storytelling with infectious melodies, Meels is quickly capturing hearts and carving out her place in today’s country music scene. 
VIP1: The Nitty Gritty Front Row with Meet & Greet Experience   $305 
Includes: 

Premium reserved ticket in the front row
Invitation to a private post-show meet & greet with the members of Nitty Gritty Dirt Band*
Exclusive signed 8×10 photo
Commemorative VIP laminate

*Not all members may be present 
VIP2: The Nitty Gritty Premium Seat with Meet & Greet Experience   $229  
Includes: 

Premium reserved ticket in first ten (10) rows
Invitation to a private post-show meet & greet with the members of Nitty Gritty Dirt Band*
Exclusive signed 8×10 photo
Commemorative VIP laminate

*Not all members may be present 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Parachute Play
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:

A great activity for young children and their adults to come together and share an exciting and engaging play experience! (Must be 18 months-younger 3 years old with an adult, adult involvement is required)Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, April 1st for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, April 3rd for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Please plan to attend and be engaged with your child. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.

title: Cancelled - Ready, Set, Go! Kindergarten Readiness
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Storytime, Children

This program is focused on developing skills to prepare children who will be entering Kindergarten in the fall of 2026. Children will attend this program independently and should not be putting things in their mouths. Adults will be required to remain in the Children's Library during the program and will be invited in the last 5 minutes to review lessons taught that day and skills to be practice at home. Please use the special notes section of the program registration to let us know if your child needs any considerations or accommodations.Be aware, this program is for 5 sessions. (April 6, 13, 20, 27 and May 4). Failure to attend the first of the series will result in forfeiting your spot to someone on the waitlist.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, March 18th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, March 20th for residents of other municipalities.Laura has two decades of experience in preschool education. She is gifted at captivating young minds with engaging and interactive content. With her knowledge and understanding of child development, she will present lessons and activities that will stimulate curiosity, foster creativity, and nurture social and emotional skills. Laura has a remarkable ability to communicate complex concepts in simple, age-appropriate ways, creating a dynamic learning environment where every child feels valued and supported. Your child will experience a memorable program that makes learning fun!

Children are invited to explore a realistic mini-grocery market complete with colorful play food, shopping baskets, cash registers, and more. They can shop, stock shelves, or take on the role of a cashier, fostering creativity, social interaction, and basic math skills in a fun, welcoming environment.This drop-in program is perfect for children ages 2–7 and their families. No registration is required—just come by and let your little ones experience the joy of imaginative play!Come play, learn, and grow at the library! Each family group must include a responsible adult in the room at all times. This program is for children 2 to 7 years old only! While other ages are welcome in the room, we kindly ask that the play area and toys remain reserved exclusively for the intended age group to ensure a safe environment for young participants.

title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated
(https://www.paypal.com/donate?token=AzIxMnj72R-pxLPP0cGQILM7OPiZs5zbsOdxGA7LFH60Wkr_0YcLATVAkIHYVjrFl4PP36X2GkDEJbaM)

It’s a year of suspense, great conversation, and maybe a few red herrings along the way.Registration is encouragedJanuary 12: The Puzzle Box by Danielle TrussoniFebruary 9: The New Girl by Daniel SilvaMarch 9: The Stolen Queen by Fiona DavisApril 13: Crime Scene by Jonathan & Jesse KellermanMay 11: Quiet Librarian by Allen EskensJune 8: Footprints of God by Greg IilesJuly 13: The 7½ Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle by Stuart TurtonAugust 10: Death & Judgement by Donna LeonSeptember 14: How to Solve Your Own Murder by Kristin PerrinOctober 12: The Secret of Secrets by Dan BrownNovember 9: All the Colors of the Dark by Christ WhitakerDecember 14: The Twelve Books of Christmas by Kate Carlisle

title: Lancaster Live & Learn Series 2026 - The William Whipper Reading Circle
location: Lancaster, LPL Board Room
description:
Community, Discussion, Adults, Seniors

The Commonwealth Monument Project returns to the Lancaster Public Library for its annual LIVE AND LEARN CHAUTAUQUA.Presented in memory of the life and legacy of Lenwood Sloan, the 2026 series is a salute to PA Abolitionist William Whipper's galvanizing Reading Society. Five famous speeches presented by colleagues of Whipper are matched to five contemporary books selected by the library staff. ➡️ Read the book prior to the meeting, then we'll listen to the corresponding speech and discuss both during our gathering.Registration is helpful, but walk-ins will not be turned away.List of books:January 13 - Black Genius: Essays on an American Legacy by Tre JohnsonFebruary 10 - The New Jim Crow: Mass Incarceration in the Age of Colorblindness by Michelle Alexander March 10 - Misbehaving at the Crossroads by Honoree Fanonne Jeffers April 14 - Between the World and Me by Ta-Nehisi Coates May 12 - Citizen: An American Lyric by Claudia Rankine List of speeches:January 13 - David Walker, 1829 - Appeal to the Colored Citizens of the World!February 10 - William Whipper, 1834 - The Slavery of IntemperanceMarch 10 - Henry Highland Garnett, 1843 - Call to RebellionApril 14 - Thomas Morris Chester, 1862 - Negro Self Respect and Pride of RaceMay 12 - Frederick Douglass, 1894 - Lessons of the Hour

title: Tabletop RPGs for Adults
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center C
description:
Community, Gaming, Adults

Join local GM Matt as he runs a variety of Tabletop Role Playing Games that rotate each month. No experience in any system is required and anyone interested in dipping their toes into the TTRPG world are welcomed and encouraged to join in on the fun!This month’s game is Dungeons and Dragons 5e. Welcome delicious adventurers to the world of Calorum!! A land where the question “What would happen if Candyland and Game of Thrones had a baby?”.Calorum is a fantasy setting like many others. Noble clans with hereditary titles wage war for petty reasons, marry off their family members to secure power and try to maintain their hold on the population they have legally bound to service. There is, however, a difference: everything is made out of food. Peasants made of pink gelatin gather licorice for their liege lord, nobles with skin of parmesan build forts out of cheesestone and bread blacksmiths carve fettucine into weapons of war. Registration is required. This group is for adults but check our calendar for other ages.

title: Spring is in the Air Moss Wall Workshop
location: Gallery Grow, 320 North Queen Street, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Design a framed moss wall with preserved moss, botanicals, and two air plants in this hands-on, guided workshop.
Unwind at the end of your day and join us for an intimate happy hour workshop with expert instruction. As always, BYOB.
Create a one-of-a-kind moss wall that brings texture, nature, and art together in a beautiful, modern way.
In this hands-on workshop, you’ll design a framed moss wall using a thoughtfully curated selection of preserved moss, dried elements, and botanical accents. Two air plants will be incorporated into your piece, adding sculptural dimension and a living touch to your finished design.
Our very own featured moss wall artist, Laura Alvis, will guide you through the creative process, offering styling tips, composition guidance, and design insight along the way. Whether your aesthetic leans minimal and modern or lush and layered, you’ll have the support to make it uniquely yours. 
            
        
                
                
    Register Now

Guests will explore bright, sunny flavors and techniques inspired by Latin and island cuisines while learning how to balance sweet, savory, and citrus elements. This class focuses on working with fresh ingredients, building layered flavors, and creating vibrant dishes that feel both playful and approachable. 
Hands on. BYOB. Ages 16 and up.  Gluten Free

location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center B
description:

Looking for a place to relax, tinker, and meet new people? The Sensory Social is a low-stress hangout for neurodivergent adults. We’ve gathered a variety of tactile and creative stations for you to explore at your own pace.Enjoy our sequin wall, motion bubblers, and fidget kits, or try your hand at the knit board and art stations. This is a self-guided, 'choose your own adventure' space where you can be yourself among peers. If you need a hand using the materials, please bring a friend or support person along. Come hang out and find what feels good!This program is designed for adult folks with IDD & autism. We want everyone to participate with their peers, regardless of motor or communication skills.  Please do not remove supplies from the room.

title: Mahjong 101
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center C
description:
Community, Gaming, Adults, Seniors

Go from "What's a Bam?" to "Mahjong!" in this five-week intensive designed for true beginners. We’ll dive into the beautiful tiles, the National Mah Jongg League card, and the fast-paced strategy that makes the game a total blast. By the final week, you’ll be playing with confidence and style!The Full-Table Commitment: Because each lesson builds directly on the last, signing up for the first session means you are committed to the full five-week journey. Please ensure you can attend every class to keep the "puzzle" together for the whole group.Schedule (1-3pm):April 16April 23April 30May 7May 14New Players Only, Please!: To keep things fair and give everyone a shot at the tiles, this course is strictly for true beginners. If you've already taken a Mahjong class at any library branch in the county, we ask that you leave this spot open for someone who hasn't had the chance to learn yet. We love your enthusiasm, but this specific table is reserved for those who are brand new to the game.

title: Spotting the Fake
location: Ephrata, EPH Conference Room
description:
Technology Class, Adults, Seniors

We will equip you with some skills and strategies needed to navigate today’s digital landscape, helping you recognize the subtle artifacts and patterns indicative of AI-generated media. Through hands-on analysis of synthetic images, deepfake videos, and algorithmically produced news, you will be able to learn techniques necessary to separate machine-made fiction from human reality. This class is supported by a grant from the OpenAI Foundation. Ages 18+.

location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:

Drop-in program. No registration required.Let your child’s imagination soar in this hands-on free play program! Designed for kids ages 2-7, this engaging session provides a creative and interactive environment where children can explore, build, and learn through play. No registration is required.Using vibrant magnetic tiles and a variety of manipulatives such as blocks, gears, and more, children will:Develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills.Enhance fine motor abilities and hand-eye coordination.Foster collaboration and social interaction with peers.This open-ended program encourages creativity, exploration, and discovery, all while having fun! Whether constructing towering structures, creating intricate patterns, or experimenting with new ideas, kids will enjoy a safe, educational experience.Each family group must include a responsible adult in the room at all times. This program is for children 2 to 7 years old only! While other ages are welcome in the room, we kindly ask that the play area and toys remain reserved exclusively for the intended age group to ensure a safe environment for young participants.

title: The Rise and Fall of the Pennsylvania Railroad Lecture
location: The Susquehanna National Heritage Area, 1706 Long Level Road, Wrightsville, Pennsylvania, United States
description:
Did you know the Pennsylvania Railroad—known as “The Pennsy”—was an industry leaderfor 122 years? 



Join local historian and train enthusiast Jim Bennett for his presentation, The Rise and Fallof the PA Railroad. This illustrated talk explores how the Pennsylvania Railroad fueledindustry and contributed to the growth and development of York and Lancaster Counties,becoming the standard by which America’s railroads were measured. 



Attendees will learn about the railroad’s ambitious beginnings, its rise to prominence, andthe factors that ultimately led to its decline. Bennett, a Lancaster County resident, hasshared his engaging train talks with numerous local groups and brings a passion for railhistory to this in-depth look at one of America’s most influential corporations.

title: Captain Henry Haines: Soldier and County Statesman
location: Columbia, COL Meeting Room
description:
Presentation, Tweens, Teens, Adults, Seniors, Everyone

Captain Henry Haines was born in Maytown, PA in 1835. He became a very prominent citizen of the community during his early years. When the outbreak of the Civil War occurred, he mustered in 1861 with the First Regiment. Haines did not sit by when he mustered out, instead, re-enlisting again in 1862. After his second enlistment, he chose to enlist for a third time. His third enlistment did not do him well as he became a Prisoner of War in early 1865. After Haines was released from prison and discharged, he moved back to Maytown and became a very prominent citizen not only of his community, but also of Lancaster County. Bio for Glenn Bachert:My name is Glenn D Bachert, I was born and raised in Columbia, and I am a graduate of Columbia High School, class of 1974.  Upon my graduation and after a few months I enlisted in the United States Air Force in 1975 and served until 1982. Having served throughout the U.S. and other military bases. In 1977, I married my high school sweetheart Carol. We been married for 49 years. We have 2 children and 2 grandchildren. I relocated back to Columbia in 2008 after living in Mount Joy for 30 years.My love of history has always been a passion of mine especially with all members of my family having served in military service.  Traveling to many Civil War Battlefields I started to dive deep into the history when I received my Great-Great Grandfather's Sabre from the Civil War.  As other individuals knew about my passion I was asked to portray Captain Henry Haines during Maytown's Mayfest many years ago.  I have given lectures on his life and military service during his time of the Civil War.Presented by Glenn Bachert with Cindy Beaston.

description:

In a world that often teaches boys to stay silent about their struggles, this session will explore the pressures young men face, the warning signs we should not ignore, and the ways families, churches, and communities can foster openness, resilience, and hope. We’ll gather over a festive Easter Punch mocktail while creating space for thoughtful dialogue and meaningful questions. Come ready for a conversation that encourages courage, compassion, and connection.

description:

Please join the waitlist as we may schedule a second session if there is sufficient demand.Block Party is an hour long free play session for preschoolers and their caregivers using our library's wooden block set, people, vehicles, animals, scarves, photographs of buildings, and other accompanying materials where preschoolers have the freedom to build and play as they wish.An adult staff member or volunteer is present to model open ended questions and other supports for the adults participating.Young children learn best when they are able to construct knowledge through meaningful play. With the materials provided children will build and experiment, manipulate objects, work with others, count and measure, talk about what they are building and building vocabulary; all activities that will help children to achieve the Pennsylvania Learning Standards for early childhood in not only STEM areas, but also in language and literacy development, physical development and social and emotional development.Block party has only three rules:1) Adult caregiver must be present in room2) No throwing blocks3) No knocking down anyone else's buildingThis program is sponsored by Reynolds & Shivery Funeral Home.

—————————————————
Presented by Mickey’s Black Box
—————————————————
The Ultimate Doors has long been celebrated as one of the most authentic and powerful Doors tribute bands in the country. Described by fans and critics as -uncannily true to the original- and -the closest you’ll get to seeing The Doors live today- the band has built its reputation on delivering a raw, electrifying, and immersive experience that captures the spirit of Jim Morrison and The Doors with uncanny accuracy. 
What sets The Ultimate Doors apart is their obsessive attention to authenticity. From the period-correct instruments and vintage gear that recreate The Doors unmistakable tones, to the styling, stage presence, and energy that mirror the original band, every detail is crafted to transport audiences back to 1968. The four musicians of The Ultimate Doors combine to create a sound so faithful that fans often remark they feel as though they’re at a real Doors concert. Its not just a tribute….its a time machine.  
Check out The Ultimate Doors: Tribute to The Doors here!

title: The World on Edge & Public Disco Porch
location: Zoetropolis Cinema Stillhouse, 112 North Water Street, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, 17603, United States
description:
Zoetropolis Cinema Stillhouse, located at 112 N. Water Street in Lancaster, will host an evening of bold and atmospheric live music on Friday, April 17, 2026, beginning at 8:00 PM, featuring Harrisburg’s The World on Edge and York ensemble Public Disco Porch.

The World on Edge is a three-piece cinematic post-rock band from Harrisburg, PA, known for widescreen instrumental music built on atmosphere, tension, and emotional storytelling. Their sound layers guitars, dynamic percussion, and high-fidelity production to create immersive sonic narratives — and audiences can look forward to new material, with a full-length album due out this summer.

York’s Public Disco Porch brings a singular vision to the stage: music rooted in the land, the history, and the people of South Central Pennsylvania. Their catalog weaves folk-metal riffage, entrancing percussion, and muscled low-end into something that feels at once like historical excavation and unhinged spiritual celebration.

Together, the two acts offer a rare evening of music that is ambitious, deeply felt, and entirely original. 
            
        
                
            
                
                
    Buy Tickets

title: Creative Release Open Studio Saturday
description:
The Creative House of Lancaster
#### ***A Place to Make Art Together***

**When:** Every Other Saturday from 10am - 12pm
**Where:** [The Candy Factory](https://share.google/nqC7VtCS6IcDeBRyg)
**Cost:** Free & Open to the Public
**Ages:** All Ages

**Upcoming Dates**
April 18, May 2, May 16, May 30, June 13, June 27, July 11, July 25, Aug 8, Aug 22, Sept 5, Sept 19, Oct 3, Oct 17, Oct 31, Nov 14, Nov 28, Dec 9, Dec 23

*Do you have creative aspirations but never seem to make the time? Do you want the companionship of others to overcome your creative blocks and “just do it”?*

Creative Release provides spaces and resources that make artmaking accessible. Whether you’re an experienced artist or just looking to explore your creative side, Creative Release is a safe place to explore your ideas with others. Bring your ongoing projects or use the art materials available.

Don't have a project at the moment? That's just fine. I’ll have materials to share.
**What to Expect:**

* A welcoming, inclusive environment
* A variety of art materials to experiment with
* Demonstrations on how to use different art materials
* The opportunity to work on your personal projects
* The chance to meet and collaborate with fellow creatives
* A moment to relax, create, and escape the usual routine

**Who Should Join?**
Anyone and everyone! Bring an idea for a card, a knitting pattern you’ve been wanting to try, a shirt that needs mending, or a full-on canvas and paints. Most of all, bring your lovely self! We believe in the power of community and creativity.

[Event Link](https://www.jodavis.art/community-events-1/creative-release)
source: Meetup: The Creative House of Lancaster

title: Your Street Trees and the Urban Forest
location: Lancaster, LPL Board Room
description:
Community, Teens, Adults, Seniors

Trees are critically important to our living in a healthy city, not just in parks and yards, but also along the streets and alleys. Through this presentation you will be introduced to the benefits of street trees, including environmental, economic, and social impacts. Learn how the City of Lancaster addresses common urban tree challenges such as pests/diseases, development, and the importance of community involvement in tree preservation. Audience members will take part in activities that will help them identify trees, how to report tree issues, and better understand the City’s municipal tree policies. By better understanding the unique environment of the City, our urban forest will remain healthy and verdant, and continue to benefit all residents and visitors long into the future.All ages · Walk-ins welcome!

location: Ephrata, EPH 1/2 Multipurpose Room Kitchen Side
description:
Arts & Crafts, Children, Tweens

We will celebrate the life and times of Courtney Moore. Courtney is the best gamer at the arcade. But she can’t understand why there aren’t more girl characters in the games she plays. When a school project allows her to create her own video game, the hero is a girl who knows how to handle any situation—something Courtney struggles with in real life. Whether it’s learning to share a room with her stepsister or supporting her mom when she announces she’s running for mayor, Courtney’s blended family has to learn to work together differently. It’s a whole new game for Courtney, and she’s figuring out the rules as she goes. We’ll have tea, snacks and a craft from that time period. Come in costume if you wish and bring your doll! Ages 8-12.

title: Folsom Prison Experience - A Johnny Cash Music/Dramatic Production
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $24 $34 $44 
Prepare to step back in time and immerse yourself in the unforgettable atmosphere of the legendary 1968 Johnny Cash show at Folsom Prison, where the spirit of that historic day springs to life. 
As the doors swing open, you’ll find yourself instantly transformed into an inmate, ready to embark on a captivating firsthand experience. Marvel at the commanding presence of Johnny Cash himself, joined by the incomparable June Carter and the melodious harmonies of the Statler Brothers. Sense the palpable tension as the watchful warden and his guards maintain order within the prison walls. 
Leading you through this extraordinary journey is the ever-serious emcee, Hugh Cherry. Get ready to be transported, as you become an integral part of this immersive tribute, capturing the raw energy and soul that made the Folsom Prison performance truly remarkable. A memorable show for all ages. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Preserving the Heart of Lancaster: A Race Against Time
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center B
description:
Community, Discussion, Presentation, Adults, Seniors

Lancaster County is famous for its rolling hills and rich agricultural heritage, but the landscape is changing fast. We currently lose over 3,000 acres of farmland every year to development. With 70% of our county's fields still at risk, the mission to protect our soil and water has never been more urgent. Join Jeff Swinehart, President and CEO of the Lancaster Farmland Trust to discover why farmland preservation is about much more than a pretty view. During this presentation, Jeff will share his 24 years of expertise to explain how the preservation process works, how it impacts our local groundwater and watersheds, and what the future looks like for our community. Whether you are a curious neighbor, a landowner exploring your options, or someone who simply wants to ensure Lancaster's agricultural roots stay firmly planted, this is your chance to see what is at stake. Come learn how we can work together to protect our home for generations to come!

location: Ephrata, EPH Conference Room

title: "The Librarians" Film Screening
location: Ephrata, EPH Multipurpose Room
description:

Drawing on historical context, “The Librarians” explores the broader implications for education and public life. Join us for an in-depth look at the fight against libraries and how book review policies are shaping their communities. Ages 16+.

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:

A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

title: An Evening With Uli Jon Roth *rescheduled date*
location: Mickey’s Black Box, 101 Rock Lititz Boulevard, Lititz, PA, 17543, United States
description:
Thursday April 23rd, 2026
Doors | 7:00 PM
Show | 8:00 PM 
Reserved Balcony & Mezzanine Seating | $49.65 + Fees
Reserved Riser Seating | $60.25 + Fees
Reserved Orchestra Seating | $70.50 + Fees 
The Uli Jon Roth US tour originally planned for fall 2025 has been rescheduled to April 2026. “Sometimes destiny calls for patience… Our US Tour moves to April 2026 – with more energy, more magic, and more music than ever before,” explains Uli Jon Roth.  
All tickets remain valid but can also be refunded at the respective points of purchase. With the new dates, Uli Jon Roth aims to offer his US fans an even more intense concert experience. Celebrating the anniversary of SCORPIONS In Trance and Virgin Killer, along with performing his deeply heartfelt work Pictures of Destiny.  
+$5 on All Day of Show Purchases
This event is all ages – 21+ With Valid ID to Drink
Come hungry! Snacks and light bites will be available at the canteen.
—————————————————
Presented by Big Fan Presents
—————————————————
EXPERIENCE AN UNFORGETTABLE MUSICAL AND ARTISTIC JOURNEY Legendary guitarist and composer Uli Jon Roth is set to return to North America with an extraordinary new program titled “Pictures of Destiny.“ 
This highly anticipated tour promises to be a mesmerizing three-hour extravaganza, including the premiere of compositions from his long-awaited Requiem for an Angel. TOUR HIGHLIGHTS Uli Jon Roth will be accompanied by a full band and a string quartet, making for an ensemble of 10 talented musicians performing on stage. The music will be enriched by dynamic multimedia projections, offering audiences an immersive experience at seated venues. Fans will have the opportunity to witness a stunning orchestral rendition of Roth’s groundbreaking piece Sails of Charon, alongside several new compositions and material from his acclaimed Transcendental Sky Guitar albums.  
There will also be a couple of classic early Scorpions tracks, from Uli’s Tokyo Tapes Revisited DVD, blending a touch of nostalgia with innovative artistry. While the show will include many beloved pieces, the spotlight will shine brightly on Roth’s latest compositions, marking their debut in the United States. ART MEETS MUSIC As a special addition, Roth will showcase a collection of his original oil paintings, inspired by his music and songs, in each venue’s foyer.  
Critics have described his artwork as “Rembrandt meets Star Wars,” and these visually striking works of art will be integrated into the performances to enhance the audience’s journey. A TRANSFORMATIVE EXPERIENCE Roth’s tour offers more than just a concert—it is an emotional and artistic odyssey. Through his exceptional guitar artistry and evocative paintings, Roth invites audiences to immerse themselves in a world of profound beauty and inspiration. Don’t miss this unique opportunity to witness Uli Jon Roth’s unparalleled fusion of music and visual art. 
Check out Uli Jon Roth here!
Website | Instagram |  Facebook

title: One Night of Queen performed by Gary Mullen & The Works
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, September 10 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, September 12 at 10:00 a.m. 
For over 20+ years Gary Mullen and The Works have been performing their world renowned One Night of Queen live concert.  The band will have you dancing in the aisles during their show while they pay tribute to the stage theatrics, showmanship, and music of Rock & Roll Hall of Fame inductees, Queen.  Gary Mullen & The Works have performed to sold-out audiences around the world, playing more than 2,500 live shows to audiences in over 20 countries – performing in many of the same venues that Queen have played at around the world.  The Show DOES go on! 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $79
 
For more than 30 years, Grammy Award-winning country band Shenandoah has delivered heartfelt songs and rich harmonies that helped shape the sound of modern country music. Led by original frontman Marty Raybon, the group rose to fame with beloved hits like “Two Dozen Roses,” “Church on Cumberland Road,” “Next to You, Next to Me,” and “I Want to Be Loved Like That.” 
Known for their genuine lyrics and timeless sound, Shenandoah continues to captivate audiences with a mix of nostalgia and new energy. Experience an evening filled with classic favorites and the unmistakable charm of one of country music’s most enduring groups.

Stayin’ Alive is the largest and most definitive production of its kind, offering big screen video clips, photos, and dazzling imagery. Stayin’ Alive is the quintessential tribute band to the Bee Gees, capturing the excitement of live performance and the tender subtleties of the human voice.

title: My Generation Tour: The Lovin Spoonful, Terry Sylvester and Gary Lewis & The Playboys
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $79 $89
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, October 1 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, October 3 at 10:00 a.m. 
“This good-time spectacular celebrates the time of our lives with all three acts performing with their own full bands spanning the golden singles-era of pop music, from 1965 to 1975. This was the magic time where hit singles ruled the charts and won our hearts,” producer Jim Della Croce exclaims. Terry Sylvester, who famously replaced Graham Nash after he left The Hollies, will perform along with famed pop legend Gary Lewis. “This show is the baby-boomer bucket list must see event of the year, a hip trip back to the era that defined our generation,” he concludes. 
Between The Lovin’ Spoonful, The Hollies, and Gary Lewis and The Playboys, the charts exploded every week with a new tune that captivated our rock and roll souls. Nearly 30 hits will explode throughout this two-hour celebration: “Daydream,” “Do You Believe in Magic,” “This Diamond Ring,” “Carrie Anne,” “Long Cool Woman (in a Black Dress), “Count Me In,” “He Ain’t Heavy, He’s My Brother,” “Summer in the City,” “She’s Just My Style,” “You Didn’t Have to Be So Nice,” “On a Carousel,”  “Sure Gonna Miss Her” and “The Air that I Breathe.”

title: Asking the Hard Questions
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
At Church of the Apostles, we know many are carrying deep and honest questions about faith, especially in light of what is happening in our country and throughout the world. Questions like “Where is God in all of this?” are not signs of weak faith, but part of a faithful journey we are called to walk together. 
To create space for these conversations, we invite you to a new monthly series, Asking the Hard Questions, hosted by our seminary intern, Deklan Lewis, in the Parlor. Together, we will explore doubt, struggle, and belief as meaningful parts of discipleship. Our first two gatherings will include viewing and discussing the film God on Trial as we wrestle, much like Jacob, with faith and what it means to follow Jesus today. 
To accommodate different schedules, two sessions will be offered each month: Monday morning, April 27th at 10 AM, and Thursday evening, April 30th at 6 PM. All are welcome to join this thoughtful, open, and supportive conversation.

title: Teen Book Talk: A Little Princess
location: Manheim Township, MTP Study Room #119
description:
Book Club, Teens

For Teens ages 13-18 years of age only.Calling all teen readers! Join us for Teen Book Talk, a fun and engaging monthly book discussion group led by members of our Teen Advisory Board! Book selections are chosen by teens during advisory meetings. Interested in joining the Teen Advisory Board? Sign up through the library calendar. Each month, we’ll dive into a new title—ranging from trending young adult fiction to graphic novels, fantasy epics, thought-provoking contemporary stories, and more.This month we'll be reading "A Little Princess" by Frances Hodgson. Sara Crewe looks like a princess as she enters boarding school - she wears beautiful clothes and has the best manners.  But what happens when all of the money disappears and Sara is left with nothing?  She is forced to sleep in the attic of the school and is wearing rags.  Can Sara overcome this "riches to rags" turn of events and come out as the princess she knows she is in the end? You’ll get the chance to share your thoughts, hear different perspectives, and connect with fellow book lovers in a relaxed and welcoming space. Snacks will be provided, and occasionally we’ll have themed activities or giveaways tied to the book!Note: To help everyone stay present and get the most out of the experience, we ask that all phones stay in pockets or bags during the program. Thank you for your cooperation!Library Program PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated

title: Lit Lovers Book Group
location: Milanof-Schock, MJY Grissinger Board Room
description:
Book Club, Adults

Lit Lovers is an eclectic reading group with a primary focus on fiction, while also exploring memoirs, short stories, biographies and autobiographies, mysteries, science fiction, and select works of nonfiction. The group is known for its lively, thoughtful discussions and makes a concerted effort to respect differing viewpoints. To keep conversations focused and meaningful, social chatter, gossip, and romance novels are generally kept outside the allotted discussion time.Lit Lovers gravitate toward modern fiction distinguished by strong writing, well-developed characters, and compelling narratives. Common themes include contemporary issues, family conflict, and the triumph of the human spirit, with a healthy appreciation for humor. Members read for both personal enrichment and enjoyment, and the group frequently selects works by international authors, books set abroad, or stories that explore multicultural perspectives.For a list of this year's book selections, click on the photo to the left or pick up a bookmark at the library!

What to Bring: Sets and racks (at least one per four players)Your current 2026 National Mah Jongg League cardA friendly, patient attitude! Community GuidelinesBe Welcoming:  We all started as beginners! Please be patient with those still learning to playThe "Rule of Five": If a fifth player joins your table, we suggest the "East Sits Out" rotation—the player in the East position rotates out for one hand so everyone gets to playFair Play:  Tables are available on a first-come, first-served basisPlease keep talk inclusive and the atmosphere kindRegistration is Required for this event

title: Spotting the Fake
location: Ephrata, EPH Conference Room
description:
Technology Class, Adults, Seniors

We will equip you with some skills and strategies needed to navigate today’s digital landscape, helping you recognize the subtle artifacts and patterns indicative of AI-generated media. Through hands-on analysis of synthetic images, deepfake videos, and algorithmically produced news, you will be able to learn techniques necessary to separate machine-made fiction from human reality. This class is supported by a grant from the OpenAI Foundation. Ages 18+.
