title: Asking the Hard Questions
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
At Church of the Apostles, we know many are carrying deep and honest questions about faith, especially in light of what is happening in our country and throughout the world. Questions like “Where is God in all of this?” are not signs of weak faith, but part of a faithful journey we are called to walk together. 
To create space for these conversations, we invite you to a new monthly series, Asking the Hard Questions, hosted by our seminary intern, Deklan Lewis, in the Parlor. Together, we will explore doubt, struggle, and belief as meaningful parts of discipleship. Our first two gatherings will include viewing and discussing the film God on Trial as we wrestle, much like Jacob, with faith and what it means to follow Jesus today. 
To accommodate different schedules, two sessions will be offered each month: Monday morning, April 27th at 10 AM, and Thursday evening, April 30th at 6 PM. All are welcome to join this thoughtful, open, and supportive conversation.

title: Leslie Odom, Jr.
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $79 $99 $109 $129
 
Leslie Odom, Jr. is a Tony and Grammy Award-winning, three-time Emmy and two-time Academy Award-nominated songwriter, actor, and a New York Times bestselling author. Well known for his breakout role as ‘Aaron Burr’ in the Pulitzer Prize-winning Broadway musical Hamilton, Odom made the season’s most highly anticipated return to his award-winning role in September 2025. During his limited engagement run, which came nine years after his final bow, Hamilton hit its highest ever gross in its ten years on Broadway. 
Odom’s long-awaited return to Broadway following his original Hamilton run came in 2023 as a producer, alongside his beloved Nicolette Robinson and the gracious Jeffrey Richards, with the classic American comedy Purlie Victorious: A Non-Confederate Romp Through the Cotton Patch written by the legendary Ossie Davis. The revival opened to widespread critical acclaim. Odom was Tony-nominated for his performance, and the production received six nominations and one win. Purlie Victorious was recorded and aired as part of PBS’ Great Performances.  
Odom has continued to captivate audiences across film and television including his star turn as legendary soul singer Sam Cooke in the Amazon film adaptation of One Night in Miami…, directed by Regina King. His lauded portrayal of the soul icon and musical performance of original song “Speak Now” earned him multiple awards and nominations. Additional film and television credits include Rian Johnson’s record-breaking 2022 Knives Out sequel, Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery, for Netflix, The Many Saints of Newark, a prequel to David Chase’s award-winning HBO series The Sopranos, Apple TV+’s Central Park (2020 Emmy nomination), Hamilton on Disney+ (2021 Emmy nomination), host of “The Tony Awards Present: Broadway’s Back!” on CBS (2022 Emmy nomination), Abbott Elementary, The Proud Family: Louder and Prouder, Harriet, and many more. 
n March 2018, Odom added the title of author to his resume with the release of his book – Failing Up: How to Take Risks, Aim Higher and Never Stop Learning, which was recently released in paperback in March 2025. Co-written with Nicolette Robinson, Odom’s first children’s book, I Love You More Than You’ll Ever Know, was published by Feiwel & Friends in March 2023. The book debuted on the New York Times bestseller list at #7 in its first week. Odom has released six full-length albums. His sixth and first-ever live album, An Offering: Live at Speakeasy Studios, was released on October 17, 2025. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

description:
It’s the moment we’ve all been waiting for: the Grand Opening of the Thaddeus Stevens & Lydia Hamilton Smith Center for History and Democracy!

title: STOMP
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $44 $54 $64 $84 
STOMP is explosive, provocative, sophisticated, sexy, utterly unique, and appeals to audiences of all ages.  The international percussion sensation has garnered an armful of awards and rave reviews and has appeared on numerous national television shows. The eight-member troupe uses everything but conventional percussion instruments – matchboxes, wooden poles, brooms, garbage cans, Zippo lighters, hubcaps – to fill the stage with magnificent rhythms.  The return of the percussive hit also brings some new surprises, with some sections of the show now updated and restructured and the addition of two new full-scale routines, utilizing props like tractor tire inner tubes and paint cans.  As USA Today says, “STOMP finds beautiful noises in the strangest places.”  STOMP.  See what all the noise is about. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Grand Opening Ribbon Cutting Ceremony
location: The Stevens & Smith Center, 13-15 East Vine Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Opening on May 1, 2026, in the heart of downtown Lancaster, the restored historic properties once owned by Thaddeus Stevens and his partner Lydia Hamilton Smith are re-envisioned as a state-of-the-art museum and education space. Innovative exhibits, created with leading historians and designers, confront slavery, freedom, and the ongoing fight for equality in America. LancasterHistory—together with many collaborative partners—are eager to welcome the public for this once-in-a-generation moment for our community, a celebration as bold as the history it honors!

The excitement kicks off Friday morning as we officially open the Stevens & Smith Center with a celebratory ribbon-cutting! Doors open at 8am for coffee, light fare, and mingling. At 9am, the program begins with inspiring remarks and special guest performances that set the stage for a powerful new chapter.

This event is free and open to the entire community; we can’t wait to celebrate with you! Please RSVP so we can ensure ample seating and plenty of breakfast items for everyone. Join us as we mark this historic moment together and open the doors to something extraordinary!

RSVP for the Ribbon Cutting

title: "Four Points of Colonization" Talk with Dr. Ann Dapice
location: Adamstown, ADM Community Room
description:
Presentation, Adults, Seniors

Join us for this special presentation by Dr. Ann Dapice. During this talk, she will be focusing on the colonization of Native Americans, as well as Native American history. See below for a short biography about Dr. Dapice's background. Registration Requested for this event.Ann Dapice received a PhD in psychology, sociology and philosophy from the University of Pennsylvania. She has taught and/or served as administrator at a number of universities teaching courses in the social sciences, philosophy and Native American Studies. She is Director of Education and Research for T.K. Wolf, Inc., a 501(c)(3) American Indian organization and Founder/Executive Director, Institute of Values Inquiry. Her cross-cultural and interdisciplinary research has been reported in professional journals, books, and academic presentations regionally, nationally and internationally—and in newspapers, radio, television, and the internet.

————————————————————– 
Friday evening culminates in an elegant, high-end cocktail celebration honoring the extraordinary generosity and bold vision that brought the Stevens & Smith Center to life. This exclusive, ticketed event brings together our closest supporters for a night of gratitude and recognition, just next door in the newly renovated Commons on Vine.

title: Mako & Haejin Fujimura
location: The Trust Performing Arts Center, 37 N. Market St., Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Sponsored by The Open Discourse Coalition, this special evening at The Trust welcomes back Makoto Fujimura, accompanied by his wife, Haejin Fujimura, for a conversation centered on their forthcoming book, “Beauty X Justice.” 
We often imagine beauty and justice as opposites: one concerned with truth and fairness, the other with aesthetics and adornment. What if they aren’t rivals at all? What if beauty and justice belong together, moving in step to create lives, communities, and cultures marked by courage and abundance? 
In this fireside-style conversation, the Fujimuras will explore how beauty and justice can work hand in hand, drawing from their shared life, creative practice, and public work. More than ideas alone, this evening offers a close look at how their vision takes shape in the real world, through art, advocacy, and cultural renewal. 
The conversation will be guided by The Trust’s Founder & Curator, Tom Becker, beneath the Beaux Arts vaulted ceiling of The Trust Performing Arts Center in downtown Lancaster. 
Bring your friends, your curiosity, and your questions. Seating is free with advanced registration. Space is limited, so reserve your tickets early! 
Reserve Your Tickets

title: Chickies Rock Warbler Walk

description:
Marie Johnston will lead a field trip to Chickies Rock County Park. Meet at 7 a.m. at Breezy View Overlook along Rt. 441 just north of Columbia. After birding Breezy View, which includes a view of a Bald Eagle’s nest on the York County side of the Susquehanna River, the group will proceed to the Chickies Rock Overlook parking lot and bird in the park on both sides of Rt. 441. The field trip will end in the day-use area of the park. In addition to warblers, target species include orioles, tanagers, and flycatchers. Be prepared for ticks and bring water and snacks/lunch. Contact Marie (717-413-2086 or mariejohnston303@gmail.com) if you have questions or by 6 p.m. on the previous day if you plan to attend. 
Meeting Location: Breezy View Overlook near 770 Breezyview Dr., Columbia, PA 17512

The morning will be spent looking for migrants and resident birds along this popular stretch of Hammer Creek. A wide variety of birds are possible with special attention paid to the warblers that use this area as their home or a brief stopover during migration. Some other target birds will include thrushes, flycatchers, tanagers, orioles and grosbeaks. This trip will last until around noontime so dress for the weather. Please contact Carl at 717-471-9969 or fledge19@verizon.net by 6 p.m. the previous day if you plan to attend.

title: Creative Release Open Studio Saturday
description:
The Creative House of Lancaster
#### ***A Place to Make Art Together***

**When:** Every Other Saturday from 10am - 12pm
**Where:** [The Candy Factory](https://share.google/nqC7VtCS6IcDeBRyg)
**Cost:** Free & Open to the Public
**Ages:** All Ages

**Upcoming Dates**
May 16, May 30, June 13, June 27, July 11, July 25, Aug 8, Aug 22, Sept 5, Sept 19, Oct 3, Oct 17, Oct 31, Nov 14, Nov 28, Dec 9, Dec 23

*Do you have creative aspirations but never seem to make the time? Do you want the companionship of others to overcome your creative blocks and “just do it”?*

Creative Release provides spaces and resources that make artmaking accessible. Whether you’re an experienced artist or just looking to explore your creative side, Creative Release is a safe place to explore your ideas with others. Bring your ongoing projects or use the art materials available.

Don't have a project at the moment? That's just fine. I’ll have materials to share.
**What to Expect:**

* A welcoming, inclusive environment
* A variety of art materials to experiment with
* Demonstrations on how to use different art materials
* The opportunity to work on your personal projects
* The chance to meet and collaborate with fellow creatives
* A moment to relax, create, and escape the usual routine

**Who Should Join?**
Anyone and everyone! Bring an idea for a card, a knitting pattern you’ve been wanting to try, a shirt that needs mending, or a full-on canvas and paints. Most of all, bring your lovely self! We believe in the power of community and creativity.

[Event Link](https://www.jodavis.art/community-events-1/creative-release)
source: Meetup: The Creative House of Lancaster

title: Mocktails & Meaning™: "The Librarians" Documentary Screening
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Make plans for a special Mocktails & Meaning™ gathering on Saturday, May 2nd at 2:30 PM in the Sanctuary as we host a screening of the documentary The Librarians. 
This powerful film follows librarians across the United States who find themselves on the front lines of debates about censorship, intellectual freedom, and access to information. Through their stories, the documentary explores what it means to defend the freedom to read in a time when books, ideas, and education are increasingly challenged in schools and communities. 
Following the screening, we will hold a Q&A and open discussion, creating space to reflect on the themes of the film and share thoughts together as a community. All are welcome for this thoughtful afternoon of film, conversation, and connection. 
Register Now By Clicking Here

title: LFS Monthly Discussion - El Serrano's
description:
Lancaster Freethought Society
Come joins us for intelligent discussion and freethinking fun at our monthly meeting. Lancaster Freethought Society is a community for Freethinkers, Atheists, Agnostics, Humanists, Non-believers, Skeptics, and those who are simply curious. There is no fee to attend our meetings which range from topical subjects of interest to freethinkers such as Church/State Separation, as well as subjects such as science, philosophy, ethics, counter-apologetics, and psychology. All are welcome to attend no matter what your beliefs may be but proselytizing is not allowed. Bring your articles, news, jokes, gripes, questions, ideas, topics, or anything else you would like to discuss with the rest of the group.

Hope to see you there!

location: Lancaster

The seminar will give you the tools you need to make informed financial decisions.Our hosts will share their expertise about practical, real-life financial topics - from saving, investing, to retirement planning, social security, as well as life insurance and disability income insurance.Thank you for registering.➡️ We will be using Zoom to host this program. You will need either a computer with a microphone and webcam, or a smartphone to connect. After you register, you will receive the meeting link via email.Sheila Stotler & Tara Hauber are registered representatives of and offers securities, investment advisory and financial planning services through MML Investors Services, LLC. title: Leonid & Friends
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $44 $54 $64 $74
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, September 24, 2025 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, September 26, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. 
With multiple sold-out U.S. tours under its belt and hundreds of thousands of fans around the world, Leonid & Friends continues to astound its global audience with its unique ability in capturing the spirit, musicality, and fire of American supergroup Chicago. 
And what is even more stunning is that Leonid & Friends, comprised of 11 of the finest musicians in Russia, haven’t seen Chicago perform live until recently, yet replicates the band’s complex arrangements amazingly note for note. Chicago has never been in Russia, and none of us have attended their concerts until just recently in the United States,” said Leonid Vorobyev, a multi-instrumentalist who is the band’s leader. “We have only recordings and videos (to help us learn Chicago songs).” 
Ironically, Vorobyev wasn’t always a fan of horn bands, and as a teenager gravitated to the hard-driving guitar-based sounds of the Beatles, Creedence Clearwater Revival, Deep Purple, and Grand Funk Railroad. “In the early 1970s in the USSR, electric guitars, amplifiers, and drums were rare to find so at school we were playing self-made instruments,” Vorobyev explained. “We were allowed to rehearse in the same room with a horns band. Those musicians almost always were drunk and quite often tried to play with us our rock songs. It was a nightmare, and I hated them all!” 
But that all changed when Vorobyev heard Chicago. He became an instant fan. “I loved so much the brilliant mixture of rock guitars, drums, and jazz horns with bright vocal harmonies,” he said.
In 2014, Vorobyev decided to indulge his love for the band by gathering a few fellow musicians in a studio to record a video of them playing his favorite Chicago tune, Brand New Love Affair. No charts or scores of the song existed so Vorobyev transcribed the tune by ear. The result was a YouTube video that quickly went viral. Since then Leonid & Friends has recorded videos of other Chicago hits including Make Me Smile, 25 or 6 to 4, and Saturday In The Park. 
All total the videos have received more than 50 million views. Also, Leonid & Friends has recorded two albums showcasing its tribute to Chicago. In September of 2018, the band recorded its first song from legendary supergroup Earth, Wind, & Fire and also paid tribute to the band Blood, Sweat & Tears as well. 
Borrowing a line from one of Chicago’s biggest hits, “it’s only the beginning” of great things from Leonid & Friends.

title: Herman's Hermits Starring Peter Noone with Chris Ruggiero
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $69 $89 
Herman’s Hermits
British Invasion icon Peter Noone—the unmistakable voice of Herman’s Hermits—brings a hit-packed evening of songs, stories, and good-natured humor. Backed by his seasoned band, Noone revisits the soundtrack of the 1960s with favorites like “I’m Into Something Good,” “Mrs. Brown, You’ve Got a Lovely Daughter,” “There’s a Kind of Hush,” “I’m Henry VIII, I Am,” “Silhouettes,” and more. Expect crisp harmonies, jangly guitars, and the playful stage presence that’s endeared him to fans across generations. Between songs, Peter shares crowd-pleasing anecdotes from the road and the heyday of the British Invasion, turning the concert into an intimate, feel-good celebration of timeless pop. Whether you grew up with these classics or you’re discovering them for the first time, this sing-along night will leave you smiling—a joyful trip back to the golden age of ’60s pop with a performer who still makes it feel brand new.
 
Chris Ruggiero
Contemporary crooner Chris Ruggiero brings the spirit of the ’60s and ’70s to life in a shimmering, feel-good concert of timeless pop. Backed by a crack band and lush arrangements, Chris leans into the romance, rhythm, and melody of the era, delivering fresh takes that honor the originals while letting his smooth, soaring vocals shine. Sing along to favorites like “This Magic Moment,” “My Cherie Amour,” “More Today Than Yesterday,” “Unchained Melody,” “Can’t Take My Eyes Off You,” and more—interwoven with charming stories about the artists and the songs that shaped a generation. With easy charisma, crisp harmonies, and plenty of heart, Ruggiero turns nostalgia into a living, breathing celebration—perfect for date night, family night, or anyone who loves great songs sung beautifully. If you treasure classic pop and soul, this uplifting evening will leave you smiling long after the final note.

title: Take Me Home: The Music of John Denver Starring Jim Curry
location: PA, United States
description:
Tickets: $29 $39 $49 
After being selected to sing as the voice of John Denver in the CBS TV movie “Take Me Home, the John Denver Story,” there was no doubt that Jim Curry would continue to sing the praises and songs of America’s Troubadour – John Denver.  As a life-long fan, Curry created the ultimate tribute experience, dedicating himself to continue Denver’s legacy of songs and his message of love and caring for our planet to future generations.  Since 2002 the combined talents of Curry and his band have brought Denver’s music to thousands of people.  Curry also works behind the scenes to preserve Denver’s music, most notably in rebuilding and preserving Denver’s original symphony arrangements. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

title: Oh Captain My Captain! for Tweens
location: Ephrata, EPH 1/2 Multipurpose Room Kitchen Side
description:
Gaming, Tweens

Oh Captain, My Captain! is a short form worldbuilding role-playing game (RPG) that explores heroism and the complicated relationships we have with people we admire. The game revolves around players drawing from a deck of cards with open-ended questions. Each question players answer helps them learn about their character, their world, their captain and the complicated relationship they have with a living legend. Players use these answers to make connections between their stories leading up one of three final questions that end the game. No experience or prep needed! Ages 10-12.

title: Lancaster Live & Learn Series 2026 - The William Whipper Reading Circle
location: Lancaster, LPL Board Room
description:
Community, Discussion, Adults, Seniors

The Commonwealth Monument Project returns to the Lancaster Public Library for its annual LIVE AND LEARN CHAUTAUQUA.Presented in memory of the life and legacy of Lenwood Sloan, the 2026 series is a salute to PA Abolitionist William Whipper's galvanizing Reading Society. Five famous speeches presented by colleagues of Whipper are matched to five contemporary books selected by the library staff. ➡️ Read the book prior to the meeting, then we'll listen to the corresponding speech and discuss both during our gathering.Registration is helpful, but walk-ins will not be turned away.List of books:January 13 - Black Genius: Essays on an American Legacy by Tre JohnsonFebruary 10 - The New Jim Crow: Mass Incarceration in the Age of Colorblindness by Michelle Alexander March 10 - Misbehaving at the Crossroads by Honoree Fanonne Jeffers April 14 - Between the World and Me by Ta-Nehisi Coates May 12 - Citizen: An American Lyric by Claudia Rankine List of speeches:January 13 - David Walker, 1829 - Appeal to the Colored Citizens of the World!February 10 - William Whipper, 1834 - The Slavery of IntemperanceMarch 10 - Henry Highland Garnett, 1843 - Call to RebellionApril 14 - Thomas Morris Chester, 1862 - Negro Self Respect and Pride of RaceMay 12 - Frederick Douglass, 1894 - Lessons of the Hour

title: Farm to Fork Experience
location: Bird-in-Hand Village Inn & Suites, 2695 Old Philadelphia Pike, Bird-in-Hand, PA, 17505, United States
description:
Real Farms • Real Food
May 13, 2026
Food doesn’t get any more authentic than Bird-in-Hand’s Farm-to-Fork Experience. From milking cows to making ice cream to growing chemical-free produce from seed, you’ll meet Mennonite and Amish farmers and get an intimate view of how Lancaster County’s bounty begins. Our own John Smucker, an eighth-generation farmer, will be your guide on this unforgettable experience, which culminates in a mouth-watering farm-to-fork meal. 
The Farm to Fork Experience package includes:
• Two-night stay in the historic Bird-in-Hand Village Inn & Suites
• Tours of local farms and a lunch made with ingredients from our tour – see itinerary below
• Daily deluxe continental breakfasts for two at the Village Inn & Suites or credit at the Bird-in-Hand Bakery & Cafe
• Dinner for two at the Bird-in-Hand Family Restaurant & Smorgasbord on Tuesday or Wednesday
• Welcome market basket filled with local foods 
Space is limited so reserve early. Enjoy this package for $585 (plus tax). Extend your stay for only $109 a night (plus tax). For more information on the Village Inn & Suites, click here. Walking is required for these tours. 
Requires $200 deposit to reserve your tour (refundable up to two weeks prior to event). $109 extra night available for any room type. Taxes not included. 
  
2026 TOUR ITINERARY 
Rise, Shine & Dine (5:30 am): Wake up with the roosters and see cows being milked at our neighbor Aaron Lantz’s organic farm. Then savor a delicious farm-fresh breakfast with the Lantz family in their kitchen. 
Hydroponic Farming: At Millcreek Hydroponics, we’ll learn about the sustainable year-round growing methods for their lettuce and microgreens, and tour their greenhouse. 
The Time is Ripe: Take an orchard tour of Kauffman’s Fruit & Farm Market and learn how they produce their apple cider. 
Veggies on the Vine: Amish farmer Ephraim Huyard will show us his biological methods of growing plants, and how he grows the chemical-free vegetable plants. 
Growing Green: Meet Wesley Groff, third-generation farmer of Groff’s Celery and Vegetable Farm, and learn how he grows crops from seed in a chemical-free environment. 
Grown Local Luncheon: You’ve seen where it’s grown. Now taste the fresh difference. Receive a market basket of local foods after lunch. 
Save Room for Dessert: Our last stop after lunch will be a visit to the Bird-in-Hand Bakery & Cafe, where we make our artisan ice cream.

We'll read some stories, do some crafts, and of course, get our hands in that soil and plant some seeds!  ***This week we'll be exploring how to plant a rainbow and we'll be painting our very own pot.  We'll also get our hands dirty as we continue to take care of our plants in the planter.***We'll supply the seeds, the tools, and the planters - you supply your own gloves.  Plan on getting a little dirty.  This program is for children ages 6 - 9 years old.  Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, April 29th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Wednesday, May 6th for residents of other municipalities.Library Program Policies No Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status. Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time. Drop Off Program – This program is intended for children over the age of 6 years who can participate independently. Adult(s) must be present to check in child with youth services staff prior to program. Adults will not be allowed in the event space.  *For children ages 6–7, this is a drop-off program; however, a parent or caregiver must remain nearby and wait outside the program room for the duration of the program. Photography The Manheim Township Public Library Code of Conduct Policy prohibits photographing or video recording individuals (including children) without their consent (or in the case of a child, the consent of a parent or guardian).

title: Gary Allan with guest Mark Mackay
location: PA, United States
description:
Tickets: $69 $79 $89 $99 
With the allure of a modern day outlaw, Gary Allan has won over fans, peers, and critics with his signature blend of smoldering vocals, rebellious lyrics, and raucous live performances. While becoming a force on the country music scene, Allan has remained true to his artistic voice each step of the way. The California native has released nine studio albums selling over 8 million albums, been certified platinum on three back-to-back albums, and been certified gold five times. Allan has five #1 hits on country radio and fourteen Top 10 hits to his credit. He has amassed over 2.8 billion total streams and has debuted at #1 on the Billboard Country Album chart four times, along with his SET YOU FREE album debuting at the top of the Billboard 200 (Pop Chart). He is described as “dark and dreamy” in Entertainment Weekly and deemed a “maverick” by Rolling Stone.  He sells out venues as a headliner from NY to LA and has appeared on The Tonight Show with Jay Leno, Late Show with David Letterman, Late Night with Jimmy Fallon, Live with Kelly and Michael, and Jimmy Kimmel Live. He has also landed on the covers of Country Weekly, Pollstar, and People magazine. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. title: Parachute Play
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:

A great activity for young children and their adults to come together and share an exciting and engaging play experience! (For safety reasons, children must not be younger than 18 months or older than 3 years old with an adult, adult involvement is required)Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, May 6th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, May 8th for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.

Go! by PD EastmanNoodle and the No-Bones Day by Jonathan GrazianoCan I Be Your Dog?  by Troy CummingsHarry the Dirty Dog by Gene ZionWhere's Spot? by Eric HillClifford the Big Red Dog by Norman BridwellSheep in a Jeep by Nancy ShawThe Landis family will be coming to our library with one of their dogs (Lily and Carly) once per month and they are excited to listen to your stories. title: Creative Release Open Studio Saturday
description:
The Creative House of Lancaster
#### ***A Place to Make Art Together***

**When:** Every Other Saturday from 10am - 12pm
**Where:** [The Candy Factory](https://share.google/nqC7VtCS6IcDeBRyg)
**Cost:** Free & Open to the Public
**Ages:** All Ages

**Upcoming Dates**
May 30, June 13, June 27, July 11, July 25, Aug 8, Aug 22, Sept 5, Sept 19, Oct 3, Oct 17, Oct 31, Nov 14, Nov 28, Dec 9, Dec 23

*Do you have creative aspirations but never seem to make the time? Do you want the companionship of others to overcome your creative blocks and “just do it”?*

Creative Release provides spaces and resources that make artmaking accessible. Whether you’re an experienced artist or just looking to explore your creative side, Creative Release is a safe place to explore your ideas with others. Bring your ongoing projects or use the art materials available.

Don't have a project at the moment? That's just fine. I’ll have materials to share.
**What to Expect:**

* A welcoming, inclusive environment
* A variety of art materials to experiment with
* Demonstrations on how to use different art materials
* The opportunity to work on your personal projects
* The chance to meet and collaborate with fellow creatives
* A moment to relax, create, and escape the usual routine

**Who Should Join?**
Anyone and everyone! Bring an idea for a card, a knitting pattern you’ve been wanting to try, a shirt that needs mending, or a full-on canvas and paints. Most of all, bring your lovely self! We believe in the power of community and creativity.

[Event Link](https://www.jodavis.art/community-events-1/creative-release)
source: Meetup: The Creative House of Lancaster

title: Dick Fox’s Golden Boys starring Frankie Avalon and Fabian with special guest Tommy Cono Tributing Bobby Rydell
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $69 $79 $89 $99 
Relive the magic of the golden era of rock ‘n’ roll with Dick Fox’s Golden Boys! This unforgettable concert event reunites beloved teen idols of the late 1950s and early 1960s — Frankie Avalon and Fabian, with special guest Tommy Cono (tributing Bobby Rydell) — for an evening packed with timeless hits, charm, and nostalgia. 
From Avalon’s chart-topping favorites like “Venus” and “Why” to Fabian’s energetic classics including “Turn Me Loose” and “Tiger,” audiences will be transported back to a time when rock ‘n’ roll ruled the airwaves. With the addition of Tommy Cono’s smooth vocals and dynamic stage presence, this show is a celebration of music that defined a generation. 
Dick Fox’s Golden Boys blends unforgettable songs, stories, and humor into a joyful trip down memory lane — reminding us why these performers became household names and fan favorites around the world. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

title: Understanding Human Trafficking in Our Community
location: Ephrata, EPH 1/2 Multipurpose Room Projector Side
description:
Community, Adults

PAATH15 Crossroads, a project of the YWCA Greater Harrisburg, is a regional network of expertly trained service providers working to combat labor and sex trafficking across Central Pennsylvania. In this engaging and informative session, Barb Bingham, Community Engagement and Training Specialist, will share insight into how human trafficking affects our communities and what we can do to address it. The presentation is designed to raise awareness, increase identification, and empower attendees to recognize and respond to trafficking situations safely and effectively. Whether you're a concerned citizen, community leader or frontline professional, this session will provide practical tools and a deeper understanding of this critical issue. Learn the signs. Understand the impact. Be part of the solution. Ages 18+.

title: Brothers of the Heart
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $69
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, August 20 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, August 22 at 10:00 a.m. 
We are excited to welcome Brothers of the Heart back to American Music Theatre! 
Super groups have a prestigious history in the music industry from such legendary rockers as The Traveling Wilburys to the iconic country foursome The Highwaymen to the stellar trio of Dolly Parton, Emmylou Harris, and Linda Ronstadt.  There’s a special excitement when successful artists combine their individual talents and star power to create something fresh and exciting.  That tradition continues with Brothers Of The Heart – Jimmy Fortune, Bradley Walker, Mike Rogers, and Ben Isaacs – who reimagine country, gospel, and pop classics with their renditions of iconic songs such as “Ring of Fire,” “Desperado,” “Bye Bye Love,” “Precious Memories,” and more. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

We’ve gathered a variety of tactile and creative stations for you to explore at your own pace.Enjoy our sequin wall, motion bubblers, and fidget kits, or try your hand at the knit board and art stations. This is a self-guided, 'choose your own adventure' space where you can be yourself among peers. If you need a hand using the materials, please bring a friend or support person along. Whether you are a seasoned researcher or just starting to wonder about your heritage, the library is here to help!Join us on the 3rd Thursday of every month at 3:00 PM for our Genealogy Drop-In Program. title: Mocktails & Meaning™: Behind the Scenes of Human Trafficking
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Join us for our May 21, 2026 Mocktails & Meaning™ gathering for a powerful and eye-opening conversation led by Brad Ortenzi, Eastern USA Regional Director of ZOE International and Coordinator of the Berks and Lancaster County Human Trafficking Task Forces. Drawing on his experience as a former Ephrata Police Detective, his work with federal and county child exploitation task forces, and his international work combating trafficking in Thailand, Brad will take us behind the scenes of human trafficking in Lancaster County. This session will explore how traffickers recruit and control victims, the psychological realities of trauma bonding, and how local task forces respond through both enforcement and prevention. Time will be set aside for questions and dialogue. We’ll be serving a Mai Tai Mocktail as we engage this important and impactful conversation. 
Register By Clicking Here

title: Parachute Play
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Storytime, Toddlers, Children

Join us for a cooperative movement program where we will use our imaginations while we play with a parachute! Stories and songs will be incorporated, while we move about and use our motor skills. A great activity for young children and their adults to come together and share an exciting and engaging play experience! (For safety reasons, children must not be younger than 18 months or older than 3 years old with an adult, adult involvement is required)Adults attending with their child(ren) do NOT need to register. Please add any additional registrants to your registration if more than one child that will be participating in the program.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, May 13th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Friday, May 15th for residents of other municipalities.Storytime PoliciesNo Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.Accompanying Adults – This program is for adults and children to enjoy together. Please plan to attend and be engaged with your child. Drop offs will not be permitted.Cancellations- Please check your email prior to departing to verify that the program has not been cancelled.Program Limits - Patrons are limited to one session of multiple session programs per week.

title: Dramatic Play Friday
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
Toddlers, Children

Drop-in program. No registration required.Join us for a fun-filled adventure at the library! Children ages 2–7 are invited to explore their creativity and imagination through hands-on pretend play. This free program offers three exciting stations:Post Office: Dress up like a mail carrier, sort mail, and deliver letters and packages just like a real mail carrier!Veterinary Office: Care for stuffed animal patients as a vet, complete with checkups and bandages.Diner: Serve up delicious imaginary meals as a chef or take orders as a friendly server.With plenty of props, costumes, and open-ended activities, your child can explore new roles, practice social skills, and have a blast.  No registration is required—just drop in and play!Each family group must include a responsible adult in the room at all times. This program is for children 2 to 7 years old only! While other ages are welcome in the room, we kindly ask that the play area and toys remain reserved exclusively for the intended age group to ensure a safe environment for young participants.

title: Block Party!
location: Quarryville, QVL Story Corner/Early Literacy Lab
description:
Community, STEM, Toddlers, Children

Registration is requested, drop ins are accommodated as space allows. Please join the waitlist as we may schedule a second session if there is sufficient demand.Block Party is an hour long free play session for preschoolers and their caregivers using our library's wooden block set, people, vehicles, animals, scarves, photographs of buildings, and other accompanying materials where preschoolers have the freedom to build and play as they wish.An adult staff member or volunteer is present to model open ended questions and other supports for the adults participating.Young children learn best when they are able to construct knowledge through meaningful play. With the materials provided children will build and experiment, manipulate objects, work with others, count and measure, talk about what they are building and building vocabulary; all activities that will help children to achieve the Pennsylvania Learning Standards for early childhood in not only STEM areas, but also in language and literacy development, physical development and social and emotional development.Block party has only three rules:1) Adult caregiver must be present in room2) No throwing blocks3) No knocking down anyone else's buildingThis program is sponsored by Reynolds & Shivery Funeral Home.

title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated

title: Lit Lovers Book Group
location: Milanof-Schock, MJY Grissinger Board Room
description:
Book Club, Adults

Lit Lovers is an eclectic reading group with a primary focus on fiction, while also exploring memoirs, short stories, biographies and autobiographies, mysteries, science fiction, and select works of nonfiction. The group is known for its lively, thoughtful discussions and makes a concerted effort to respect differing viewpoints. To keep conversations focused and meaningful, social chatter, gossip, and romance novels are generally kept outside the allotted discussion time.Lit Lovers gravitate toward modern fiction distinguished by strong writing, well-developed characters, and compelling narratives. Common themes include contemporary issues, family conflict, and the triumph of the human spirit, with a healthy appreciation for humor. Members read for both personal enrichment and enjoyment, and the group frequently selects works by international authors, books set abroad, or stories that explore multicultural perspectives.For a list of this year's book selections, click on the photo to the left or pick up a bookmark at the library!

title: Kids' Garden Club
location: Manheim Township, MTP Patio
description:
Storytime, Children

Spring has sprung and it's time for some garden fun!  Join us on our patio for our exciting garden time!  (Rain or shine - if it's raining, we'll be meeting indoors and limit our outside time.)  We'll read some stories, do some crafts, and of course, get our hands in that soil and plant some seeds!  ***This week we'll take one final look at seeds with my favorite book "The Bad Seed".  We'll create our very own "Bad Seed" and of course, we'll get our hands dirty in our pots tending to our plants.***We'll supply the seeds, the tools, and the planters - you supply your own gloves.  Plan on getting a little dirty.  This program is for children ages 6 - 9 years old.  Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, May 13th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Wednesday, May 20th for residents of other municipalities.Library Program Policies No Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status. Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time. Drop Off Program – This program is intended for children over the age of 6 years who can participate independently. Adult(s) must be present to check in child with youth services staff prior to program. Adults will not be allowed in the event space.  *For children ages 6–7, this is a drop-off program; however, a parent or caregiver must remain nearby and wait outside the program room for the duration of the program. Photography The Manheim Township Public Library Code of Conduct Policy prohibits photographing or video recording individuals (including children) without their consent (or in the case of a child, the consent of a parent or guardian).

title: Lee Brice

description:
Tickets: $99 $109 $119 $139
 
Get ready for an unforgettable night of country music when Lee Brice takes the stage at American Music Theatre! Known for his powerhouse vocals, heartfelt storytelling, and chart-topping hits like “Hard to Love,” “I Don’t Dance,” “One of Them Girls,” and “Rumor,” Brice delivers a show packed with energy, emotion, and authenticity. 
A multi-platinum artist, songwriter, and CMA award-winner, Lee Brice brings together the raw honesty of classic country with a modern edge that resonates with fans of all ages. Whether he’s performing tender ballads or high-energy anthems, his passion and stage presence make every song hit home.

Find handcrafted and vintage items, antiques and more at this special shopping event! 
Shop ‘til you drop, then dine—all without leaving our village. Artisan Village is teaming up with Creative Rustic Furniture, Modish Lancaster and Downingtown Vintage at Bird-in-Hand to bring you an outstanding day of treasure hunting in our village. Artisans from throughout the region and beyond will offer a wide variety of handcrafted merchandise. You’ll also be able to browse a broad selection of antiques and vintage items, like jewelry, clothing, décor and much more. 
In addition to the village shops, you’ll find open-air vendors in front of and behind Artisan Village, along the main walkway and around the parking lots and grassy areas near the other participating shops. Plus, you’ll be within easy walking distance of Bird-in-Hand Farmers Market and the Bird-in-Hand Bakery & Cafe – so plan to join us and make a day of it.

title: Creative Release Open Studio Saturday
description:
The Creative House of Lancaster
#### ***A Place to Make Art Together***

**When:** Every Other Saturday from 10am - 12pm
**Where:** [The Candy Factory](https://share.google/nqC7VtCS6IcDeBRyg)
**Cost:** Free & Open to the Public
**Ages:** All Ages

**Upcoming Dates**
June 13, June 27, July 11, July 25, Aug 8, Aug 22, Sept 5, Sept 19, Oct 3, Oct 17, Oct 31, Nov 14, Nov 28, Dec 9, Dec 23

*Do you have creative aspirations but never seem to make the time? Do you want the companionship of others to overcome your creative blocks and “just do it”?*

Creative Release provides spaces and resources that make artmaking accessible. Whether you’re an experienced artist or just looking to explore your creative side, Creative Release is a safe place to explore your ideas with others. Bring your ongoing projects or use the art materials available.

Don't have a project at the moment? That's just fine. I’ll have materials to share.
**What to Expect:**

* A welcoming, inclusive environment
* A variety of art materials to experiment with
* Demonstrations on how to use different art materials
* The opportunity to work on your personal projects
* The chance to meet and collaborate with fellow creatives
* A moment to relax, create, and escape the usual routine

**Who Should Join?**
Anyone and everyone! Bring an idea for a card, a knitting pattern you’ve been wanting to try, a shirt that needs mending, or a full-on canvas and paints. Most of all, bring your lovely self! We believe in the power of community and creativity.

[Event Link](https://www.jodavis.art/community-events-1/creative-release)

title: Lou Gramm: The Voice of Foreigner
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $59 $69 $79 $89
 
Get ready to relive the soundtrack of the late ’70s and ’80s when Lou Gramm, the legendary original lead singer of Foreigner, takes the stage at American Music Theatre! With one of rock’s most unmistakable voices, Gramm’s powerful vocals helped define an era, fronting chart-topping hits like “I Want to Know What Love Is,” “Cold as Ice,” “Hot Blooded,” “Juke Box Hero,” and “Double Vision.” 
In this electrifying live concert, Lou and his band deliver the energy, passion, and powerhouse vocals that made Foreigner one of the most successful rock bands of all time. Fans can expect a night packed with classics, deep cuts, and maybe a few surprises from Lou’s acclaimed solo career — including his hit “Midnight Blue.” 
Experience the timeless voice, the unforgettable songs, and the rock ’n’ roll spirit that continue to inspire generations. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. HarrowAugust 4: “The Sniper” by Liam O’FlahertySeptember 1: “How to Pronounce Knife,” “Chick-A-Chee,” and “Edge of the World” by Souvankham ThammavongsaOctober 6: “The Summer People” by Shirley JacksonNovember 3: “The Lottery Ticket” by Anton Chekhov and “Contents of the Dead Man’s Pocket” by Jack FinneyDecember 1: “The Burglar’s Christmas” by Willa Cather (under the pseudonym “Elizabeth L. Seymour”)For assistance finding copies of each story, please contact the library at 717-394-2651 ext. 1350 or askus@lancasterpubliclibrary.org.Registration is encouraged but not required!

title: A World in a Bottle: Terrarium History, Assembly, & Care
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center B
description:
Community, Health & Wellness, Presentation, Adults, Seniors

Did you know that in the 1800s, terrariums were "survival pods" used to transport tea plants across the ocean? Join us to discover how these glass enclosures changed the world-- and how they can bring a self-sustaining slice of nature into your home today!What You’ll Learn:The Foundation: Essential prep steps to take before you start building.The Kit: A guide to the specific supplies needed for a thriving ecosystem.The Build: Master the art of layering and assembly.The Longevity: Pro-tips for long-term care and quick fixes for common plant issues.Whether you have a green thumb or a history of unintentional plant homicide, this session will give you the skills to maintain a miniature world that practically takes care of itself 🌿Presented by the Penn State Extension Master Gardeners

title: Dungeons and Dragons Adventure Club for Kids (Ages 9-12 only)
location: Manheim Township, MTP Morgan Center C
description:
Gaming, Children, Tweens

To ensure fair access for as many families as possible, please read the registration guidelines for this program.One Session Per Month: Patrons may register for only one session of this program per month.Duplicate Registrations: Registrations for more than one session in the same month will be cancelled.We appreciate your understanding as we work to provide equitable access to this popular program.Calling all young adventurers ages 9 to 12! Join us for an exciting journey into the world of Dungeons & Dragons, where your imagination sets the course. Together, we’ll learn how to create unique characters, navigate magical lands, and solve thrilling quests. No prior experience is needed—new and experienced players alike are welcome!In each session, you'll explore stories full of mystery, monsters, and more all while working as a team to overcome challenges and puzzles. Whether you're a brave warrior, a clever wizard, or a sneaky rogue, there’s always an adventure waiting. Are you ready to roll the dice and discover where the story leads?Spaces are limited, so sign up today to secure your spot in our grand adventure! All materials will be provided.Registration will open at 9:00AM on Wednesday, May 20th for Manheim Township and East Lampeter residents and on Wednesday, May 27th for residents of other municipalities.LIBRARY PROGRAM POLICIES:Drop Off Program – This program is intended for children over the age of 9 years who can participate independently. Adult(s) must be present to check in child with youth services staff prior to program. Adults will not be allowed in the event space. Designated Ages – Please adhere to the age restrictions designated on your registration. Only children in that age range will be admitted.Registration Requirements –  To help us manage our space, please register every person who will be attending, including siblings.No Show – Patrons failing to cancel their registration for more than 2 library programs in a month will be restricted from registering for future programs for a period of one month’s time. Please cancel your registrations at least 2 hours prior to the start of the program to allow for those on the waiting list to be notified of their change in registration status.Late Arrivals – Please arrive 10 minutes prior to the start of the program to allow for parking and other circumstances. The program will begin promptly at the designated time. At this time, your spot will be forfeited to those on the wait list who will be granted entry. If spots remain, registrants may be allowed into the program. However, registrants will not be permitted to enter the program 5 minutes past the start time.

title: LFS Monthly Discussion - El Serrano's
description:
Lancaster Freethought Society
Come joins us for intelligent discussion and freethinking fun at our monthly meeting. Lancaster Freethought Society is a community for Freethinkers, Atheists, Agnostics, Humanists, Non-believers, Skeptics, and those who are simply curious. There is no fee to attend our meetings which range from topical subjects of interest to freethinkers such as Church/State Separation, as well as subjects such as science, philosophy, ethics, counter-apologetics, and psychology. All are welcome to attend no matter what your beliefs may be but proselytizing is not allowed. Bring your articles, news, jokes, gripes, questions, ideas, topics, or anything else you would like to discuss with the rest of the group.

Hope to see you there!

Looking for a place to relax, tinker, and meet new people? The Sensory Social is a low-stress hangout for neurodivergent adults. We’ve gathered a variety of tactile and creative stations for you to explore at your own pace.Enjoy our sequin wall, motion bubblers, and fidget kits, or try your hand at the knit board and art stations. This is a self-guided, 'choose your own adventure' space where you can be yourself among peers. If you need a hand using the materials, please bring a friend or support person along. Come hang out and find what feels good!This program is designed for adult folks with IDD & autism. We want everyone to participate with their peers, regardless of motor or communication skills.  Please do not remove supplies from the room.

title: Wee Build Free Play
location: Manheim Township, MTP Lapp Story Silo
description:
STEM, Toddlers, Children

Drop-in program. No registration required.Let your child’s imagination soar in this hands-on free play program! Designed for kids ages 2-7, this engaging session provides a creative and interactive environment where children can explore, build, and learn through play. No registration is required.Using vibrant magnetic tiles and a variety of manipulatives such as blocks, gears, and more, children will:Develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills.Enhance fine motor abilities and hand-eye coordination.Foster collaboration and social interaction with peers.This open-ended program encourages creativity, exploration, and discovery, all while having fun! Whether constructing towering structures, creating intricate patterns, or experimenting with new ideas, kids will enjoy a safe, educational experience.Each family group must include a responsible adult in the room at all times. This program is for children 2 to 7 years old only! While other ages are welcome in the room, we kindly ask that the play area and toys remain reserved exclusively for the intended age group to ensure a safe environment for young participants.

Lancaster County Chooses Love is partnering with the Lancaster Public Library to provide a safe and supportive space for LGBTQIA+ individuals and their families during drop-in office hours. During these informal office hours, an advisor from Lancaster County Chooses Love will be available in one of our private study rooms for patrons to ask questions and learn about LGBTQIA+ issues and experiences.   PLEASE NOTE: These office hours are a learning opportunity and are NOT a substitute for counseling. Lititz Chooses Love advisors are not trained mental health professionals and cannot offer professional counseling.All ages* · No registration is required · Walk-ins welcome *Per Lancaster Public Library policy, ages 11 and younger must be accompanied, at all times, by a caregiver who is 14 or older.

title: Scotty McCreery Live in Concert
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $59 $79 $99 $109
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, January 7 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, January 9 at 10:00 a.m. 
With his warm baritone, easy charm, and authentic country roots, Scotty McCreery has become one of today’s most beloved voices in country music. His chart-topping albums, multi-platinum singles, and heartfelt storytelling have earned him a devoted following and a standout place on the country music landscape. 
Fans will hear favorites like “Five More Minutes,” “Damn Strait,” and “This Is It,” along with the sincerity and energy that define Scotty’s live performances. His blend of tradition, contemporary style, and personal, relatable lyrics creates a concert experience that resonates long after the last note. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: The Righteous Brothers: Lovin' Feelin' Farewell Tour
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $69 $79 
Prepare for a soul-stirring, nostalgic concert experience at the Lovin’ Feelin’ Farewell Tour of the legendary Righteous Brothers. The Righteous Brothers Lovin’ Feelin’ Farewell Tour promises a captivating musical odyssey, spanning the early days of rock and roll, the era of Blue-Eyed Soul and as far reaching as the movie Dirty Dancing. From heartwarming ballads to energetic rock anthems, every performance is a testament to the enduring power of The Righteous Brothers’ music and their connection to fans across the globe. 
Take a journey through decades of timeless hits that have defined a generation, including “You’ve Lost That Lovin’ Feelin,'” “Unchained Melody,” “(I’ve Had) The Time of My Life,” “Soul and Inspiration,” “Ebb Tide,” and “Rock and Roll Heaven.” 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated

title: Creative Release Open Studio Saturday
description:
The Creative House of Lancaster
#### ***A Place to Make Art Together***

**When:** Every Other Saturday from 10am - 12pm
**Where:** [The Candy Factory](https://share.google/nqC7VtCS6IcDeBRyg)
**Cost:** Free & Open to the Public
**Ages:** All Ages

**Upcoming Dates**
June 27, July 11, July 25, Aug 8, Aug 22, Sept 5, Sept 19, Oct 3, Oct 17, Oct 31, Nov 14, Nov 28, Dec 9, Dec 23

*Do you have creative aspirations but never seem to make the time? Do you want the companionship of others to overcome your creative blocks and “just do it”?*

Creative Release provides spaces and resources that make artmaking accessible. Whether you’re an experienced artist or just looking to explore your creative side, Creative Release is a safe place to explore your ideas with others. Bring your ongoing projects or use the art materials available.

Don't have a project at the moment? That's just fine. I’ll have materials to share.
**What to Expect:**

* A welcoming, inclusive environment
* A variety of art materials to experiment with
* Demonstrations on how to use different art materials
* The opportunity to work on your personal projects
* The chance to meet and collaborate with fellow creatives
* A moment to relax, create, and escape the usual routine

**Who Should Join?**
Anyone and everyone! Bring an idea for a card, a knitting pattern you’ve been wanting to try, a shirt that needs mending, or a full-on canvas and paints. Most of all, bring your lovely self! We believe in the power of community and creativity.

[Event Link](https://www.jodavis.art/community-events-1/creative-release)
source: Meetup: The Creative House of Lancaster

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

title: Jo Dee Messina with special guests The Bennett Hall Band
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $69 $89
 
With powerhouse vocals, undeniable stage presence, and a string of chart-topping hits, Jo Dee Messina remains one of country music’s most vibrant and beloved artists. From the moment she burst onto the scene with “Heads Carolina, Tails California,” Jo Dee has delivered anthems that defined an era and continue to connect with fans across generations. 
Her impressive career includes timeless favorites like “I’m Alright,” “Bye-Bye,” “Lesson in Leavin’,” and “Stand Beside Me,” along with multiple Platinum albums and CMA and ACM Award wins. Known for her high-energy shows and heartfelt storytelling, Jo Dee’s live performances are a celebration of life, love, and resilience. 
The Bennett Hall Band is a rising country trio made up of sisters Kat, Presley, and Josie Hall, whose musical journey began at a young age and continues to captivate audiences across the country. Named in honor of their brother Bennett, the band blends heartfelt storytelling, tight sibling harmonies, and impressive musicianship into an authentic and energetic live performance. 
Kat and Presley began singing together as young children, quickly developing a natural harmony that became the foundation of their sound. Kat soon added guitar and songwriting, while Presley’s perfect pitch found a home on the keys. The youngest sister, Josie, brings vibrant energy on drums, ukulele, and percussion, rounding out the trio’s dynamic stage presence. 
With experience performing on renowned stages throughout Tennessee and beyond—including CMA Fest, The Station Inn, and the Franklin Main Street Festival—The Bennett Hall Band is quickly making its mark on the country music scene with talent, heart, and a sound well beyond their years.

Lauren Daigle is a multi-GRAMMY®-winning, chart-topping vocalist whose powerful, soulful sound has captivated audiences around the globe. With more than a billion streams worldwide and sold-out tours spanning North America, Europe, Australia, and Africa, she has graced some of the world’s most iconic stages, from Carnegie Hall and Red Rocks to Radio City Music Hall, the New Orleans Jazz Fest, and Lollapalooza. She has also performed on one of the world’s biggest stages, singing “America the Beautiful” at the Super Bowl alongside Trombone Shorty. 
Her breakthrough album Look Up Child (2x platinum) debuted at No. 3 on the Billboard Top 200 and made history as Daigle became the first female artist to simultaneously land in the Top 10 of both the Pop and Christian album charts. The album’s 6x platinum hit single “You Say” became one of the longest-running No. 1 songs in Billboard chart history. 
Daigle’s GRAMMY®-nominated self-titled album features standout tracks including “Thank God I Do,” “These Are the Days,” and “Be Okay,” and fueled her wildly successful Kaleidoscope Tour and Kaleidoscope Nights, which played to packed arenas and was extended by popular demand. Beyond the music, Daigle is known for her commitment to giving back through her nonprofit, The Price Fund, which supports organizations focused on music education, hunger relief, and care for the elderly—making her impact felt both on and off the stage.

title: Lancaster County Benefit Auction
location: Leola Produce Auction, 135 Brethren Church Road, Leola, PA, 17540, United States
description:
Experience the vibrant spirit of the annual Lancaster County Benefit Auction, a day where community fellowship meets the thrill of the auction block. This local tradition brings people together for a memorable experience filled with lively bidding and incredible food, all organized to benefit families facing rare genetic disorders at the Clinic for Special Children. 
The auction features an expansive array of goods ranging from traditional carriages and carts to masterfully crafted quilts and household furniture. Attendees can browse a massive inventory of farm supplies, hardware, and professional-grade tools, alongside outdoor furniture and a lush selection of shrubbery and plants. Whether you are searching for rare collectibles, children’s toys, or beautifully arranged theme baskets, the variety ensures there is something for every interest.

Click Here for Artist Presale: 
If the phrase “male a cappella group” conjures up an image of students in blue blazers, ties, and khakis singing traditional college songs on ivied campuses… think again. Straight No Chaser (SNC) are neither strait-laced nor straight-faced, but neither are they vaudeville-style kitsch. They have emerged as a phenomenon with a massive fanbase and a long list of accomplishments including 2 RIAA Gold Certified albums, over 1.5 million concert tickets sold, over 1 billion streams on Pandora, and over 2 million albums sold worldwide. Straight No Chaser is the real deal, the captivating sound of nine unadulterated human voices coming together to make extraordinary music that is moving people in a fundamental sense… and with a sense of humor.

title: Lancaster County Carriage & Antique Auction
location: Bird-in-Hand Events Field, 2727 Old Philadelphia Pike, Bird-in-Hand, PA, 17505, United States
description:
The Lancaster County Carriage & Antique Auction is a premier event that takes place annually on the Thursday & Friday before the last Saturday in June. There is an Early Bird Auction on Thursday afternoon and an all-day auction on Friday. 
Thousands of people come to the village of Bird-in-Hand for the auction which is held on the Smucker Homestead. Equine and antique enthusiasts, consignors, local residents, out-of-town visitors, and whole families look forward to our auction each year. Many are from the East Coast, but also from places as far away as Texas and California. 
This is a significant fundraiser for our Hand-in-Hand Fire Company. We are appreciative of the amazing support we receive both from our local volunteers and the visitors to our area who come back year after year.

title: Creative Release Open Studio Saturday
description:
The Creative House of Lancaster
#### ***A Place to Make Art Together***

**When:** Every Other Saturday from 10am - 12pm
**Where:** [The Candy Factory](https://share.google/nqC7VtCS6IcDeBRyg)
**Cost:** Free & Open to the Public
**Ages:** All Ages

**Upcoming Dates**
July 11, July 25, Aug 8, Aug 22, Sept 5, Sept 19, Oct 3, Oct 17, Oct 31, Nov 14, Nov 28, Dec 9, Dec 23

*Do you have creative aspirations but never seem to make the time? Do you want the companionship of others to overcome your creative blocks and “just do it”?*

Creative Release provides spaces and resources that make artmaking accessible. Whether you’re an experienced artist or just looking to explore your creative side, Creative Release is a safe place to explore your ideas with others. Bring your ongoing projects or use the art materials available.

Don't have a project at the moment? That's just fine. I’ll have materials to share.
**What to Expect:**

* A welcoming, inclusive environment
* A variety of art materials to experiment with
* Demonstrations on how to use different art materials
* The opportunity to work on your personal projects
* The chance to meet and collaborate with fellow creatives
* A moment to relax, create, and escape the usual routine

**Who Should Join?**
Anyone and everyone! Bring an idea for a card, a knitting pattern you’ve been wanting to try, a shirt that needs mending, or a full-on canvas and paints. Most of all, bring your lovely self! We believe in the power of community and creativity.

[Event Link](https://www.jodavis.art/community-events-1/creative-release)
source: Meetup: The Creative House of Lancaster

title: LFS Monthly Discussion - El Serrano's
description:
Lancaster Freethought Society
Come joins us for intelligent discussion and freethinking fun at our monthly meeting. Lancaster Freethought Society is a community for Freethinkers, Atheists, Agnostics, Humanists, Non-believers, Skeptics, and those who are simply curious. There is no fee to attend our meetings which range from topical subjects of interest to freethinkers such as Church/State Separation, as well as subjects such as science, philosophy, ethics, counter-apologetics, and psychology. All are welcome to attend no matter what your beliefs may be but proselytizing is not allowed. Bring your articles, news, jokes, gripes, questions, ideas, topics, or anything else you would like to discuss with the rest of the group.

Hope to see you there!

title: Three Dog Night
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $69 $79
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, January 21 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, January 23 at 10:00 a.m. 
Legendary band, THREE DOG NIGHT, now in its 6th decade, claims some of the most astonishing statistics in popular music. In the years 1969 through 1974, no other group achieved more top 10 hits, moved more records, or sold more concert tickets than THREE DOG NIGHT. 
THREE DOG NIGHT hits weave through the fabric of pop culture today, whether on the radio where they are heard day in and day out, in TV commercials or in major motion pictures — songs like “Mama Told Me (Not To Come)”, “Joy to the World”, “Black and White”, “Shambala” and “One” serve to heighten our emotions and crystallize THREE DOG NIGHT’s continuing popularity. 
Boasting chart and sales records that remain virtually unmatched in popular music, THREE DOG NIGHT holds a Billboard Magazine record for releasing 21 consecutive Top 40 hits, including 3 #1 singles “Mama Told Me (Not to Come)”, “Joy to the World”, and “Liar; the chart success drove 7 Platinum-selling singles and 12 straight RIAA Certified Gold LPs. The hits appeared on best-selling charts in all genres (pop, rock, rhythm & blues, and country). Its records continue to sell around the world, reaching beyond the borders of the U.S. into Japan, Canada, Holland, England, Germany, Spain and elsewhere. Tens of millions of THREE DOG NIGHT records have been sold through the years.
